[12] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_reg_ck_en_F output clk enable
[11] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_reg_ck_en_d4_F output clk div 4 enable
[10:8] R 000 RSVD
[7] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_reg_xtal_manual_mode_F reg_xtal_maunal_mode. Default=1 0: reg_xtal_sel<3:0> is controlled by REG, xtal_div_sel is controlled by mapping table. 1: reg_xtal_sel<3:0>=4'b1111, xtal_div_sel is controlled by reg_xtal_div_sel_maunal<1:0>
[6:5] R 00 B_BE_GMAC_reg_xtal_div_sel_manual_F input divider selection in xtal maunal mode. Default=00 00=/1, 10=/2, 11=/4
40MHz XTAL=00
50MHz XTAL=00
60MHz XTAL=00
80MHz XTAL=10

18600500h R_BE_DBG_SYS_CFG
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DBG_SYS_CFG Debug system config
18600800h R_BE_DCPU_PORT_PC_SET
[31:1] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_PORT_PC_SET DATACPU Port program counter setting
1 : 0x3FC[31:0] is DCPU port1 counter
0 : 0x3FC[31:0] is DCPU port0 counter
18600804h R_BE_DCPU_PORT_PC
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_PORT_PC DATACPU port1/port0 program counter
18600808h R_BE_DCPU_SYS_CLK_CTRL
[31:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_IDMEM_CLK_EN DATACPU IDMEM Clock Enable, 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_CLK_EN DATACPU Clock Enable, 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[13:0] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
18600820h R_BE_DCPU_SRAM_CTRL
[31:22] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[21] R/W 0 B_BE_r_SYM_DCPUPON_P3_PC_EN
[20] R/W 0 B_BE_r_SYM_DCPUPON_P2_PC_EN
[19] R/W 0 B_BE_r_SYM_DCPUPON_P1_PC_EN
[18] R/W 0 B_BE_r_SYM_DCPUPON_PC_EN
[17:1] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_PON_ISO_CTRL
18600860h R_BE_HALT_H2D_CTRL
31:1 R/W 0 RSVD
0 R/W 0 B_BE_HALT_H2D_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger HALT H2D REG interrupt to FW.
(After receiving H2D, FW must write 0 to B_BE_HALT_H2D_TRIGGER to let the host know the data has been received)
18600864h R_BE_HALT_D2H_CTRL
31:1 R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
0 R/W 0 B_BE_HALT_D2H_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger HALT D2H REG interrupt to the host.
(After receiving HALT D2H, the host must write 0 to B_BE_HALT_D2H_TRIGGER to let FW know the data has been received)
18600868h R_BE_HALT_H2D
31:0 R/W 0 B_BE_HALT_H2D HALT H2DREG data
1860086Ch R_BE_HALT_D2H
[31:28] R/W 0 B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO Data CPU Senario
1: RXI-300 Error
2: CPU Exception
3: Assertion Function
4.WDT
[27:16] R/W 0 RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_CODE Error code:
When B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0001' ,the field means RXI-300 Error Code
When B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0002',the field means CPU ExcCode (CoProcessor General Register Address13)
When B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0003',the field means FW Alarm Code.
18600888h R_BE_DCPU_PLATFORM_ENABLE
10 R/W 1 B_BE_DCPU_SYM_DPLT_MEM_MUX_EN Enable DCPU platform IDMEM MUX
0: disable
1: enable;
9 R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_WARM_EN DATA_CPU Core Warm reset enable bit
0: plat_reset will assert cpu_cold_reset only
1: plat_reset will assert cpu_warm_reset only
8 R/W 0 RSVD
7 R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UART_EN UART enable bit
0: UART reset
1: UART enable
6 R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_IDDMA_EN IDDMA enable bit
0: IDDMA reset
1: IDDMA enable
5 R/W 0 RSVD
4 R/W 0 RSVD
3 R/W 0 RSVD
2 R/W 1 B_BE_DCPU_APB_WRAP_EN APB_WRAP enable bit
0: APB_WRAP reset
1: APB_WRAP enbale
1 R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_EN DATA_CPU Core enable bit
0: DATA_CPU reset
1: DATA_CPU enable
0 R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_PLATFORM_EN DATA_CPU platform enabled bit
0: Platform reset
1: Platform enable
18600890h R_BE_DCPU_FWS0IMR
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_HALT_H2D_INT_EN Enable HALT H2D(triggered by0x0860) interrupt
[30:0] R/W 0 RSVD
18600894h R_BE_DCPU_FWS0ISR
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_HALT_H2D_INT HALT H2D(triggered by0x0860) interrupt
[30:0] R/W 0 RSVD
186008C0h R_BE_DATA_CPU_APB_AUTO_FIX_HANG
[31:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_DATA CPU_APB_AUTO_FIX_HANG_EN
[14:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DATA CPU_APB_AUTO_FIX_HANG_THD
186008D0h R_BE_DCPU_UDM0
[31:24] R/W 0 RSVD
[23:8] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FS_CODE FW state code: the code is defined by fw .
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_NULL_POINTER_INDC Null pointer Indicator
0: null pointer event never happened
1: null pointer event ever happened
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_ASSERTCALLED_INDC AssertCalled Indicator
0: vAssertCalled never happened
1: vAssertCalled ever happened
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_ASSERT_INDC Assert Indicator(except null pointer)
0: vAssert never happened
1: vAssert ever happened
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FW_IMAGE_TYPE FW image type
0: Normal FW
1: Wowlan FW
[3] R/W 0 RSVD
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_TRAP_LOOP_CTRL The bit control the error senario will send haltc2h or not.The bit only effect when the below 3 conditions are true at the same time:
B_BE_HALT_C2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0011'b and B_BE_UDM0_DBG_MODE_CTRL= 1'b and B_BE_UDM0_Send_HALTC2H_CTRL = 1'b
0:Send haltc2h
1:Will not send haltc2h
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_SEND_HALTD2H_CTRL The bit control the error senario will send haltd2h or not.The bit only effect in the condition:Error type:Assertion and B_BE_DCPU_DBG_MODE_CTRL=1'b1
0:Send haltd2h
1:Will not send haltd2h
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_DBG_MODE_CTRL The bit control current mode is debug or normal mode.If debug mode,FW will dump information into share buffer
0: Normal mode
1: Debug mode
186008D4h R_BE_DCPU_UDM1
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_ERROR_ADDR The address that been accessed and cause bus error or cpu exception
If B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0001'b , it means RXI300 Error Log Register
If B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0010'b , it means CPU BadVAddr(Coprocessor 0 General Register Addr=8)
If B_BE_HALT_D2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0011'b , it's meanlingness
186008D8h R_BE_DCPU_UDM2
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_EPC_RA The address of the next instruction to be executed following return from the exception handler or subroutine.
If B_BE_HALT_C2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0001'b/0011'b , it means RA.
If B_BE_HALT_C2H_ERROR_SENARIO = 0010'b , it means Return EPC
186008DCh R_BE_DCPU_UDM3
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UDM3 User Defined Message
186008E0h R_BE_DCPU_UDM4
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UDM4 User Defined Message
186008E4h R_BE_DCPU_UDM5
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UDM5 User Defined Message
186008E8h R_BE_DCPU_UDM6
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UDM6 User Defined Message
186008ECh R_BE_DCPU_UDM7
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_UDM7 User Defined Message
186008F0h R_BE_DCPU_FW_CTRL
[31] R/W 0 RSVD
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_ROM_CUT_REQ IROM write ROM cut id to B_BE_DCPU_ROM_CUT and then clear this bit if this bit is set
[29:28] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FW_ENV FW Environment
2'b00: ASIC
2'b01: FPGA
2'b10: PXP
2'b11: rsvd
[27:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_ROM_CUT IROM write ROM cut id to this byte if B_BE_DCPU_ROM_CUT_REQ is set
[7:5] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FWDL_STS DCPU fw download status, set by DCPU. (For SW handshake)
FWDL procedure is done if this symbol value is equal to DCPU_FW_INIT_RDY.
2'b111 : DCPU_FW_INIT_RDY
2'b110 : DCPU_FWDL_RDY
2'b101 : rsvd
2'b100 : CUT_NOT_MATCH
2'b011 : SECURITY_FAIL
2'b010 : CHECKSUM_FAIL
2'b001 : FWDL_ONGOING
2'b000 : INITIAL_STATE
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FW_RAM_RUN_IND This bit is set to 1 to means FW RAM code is running.
[3] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] RW 0 B_BE_DCPU_FWDL_PATH_RDY This bit is set to 1 to means that AXIDMA_CH1 is realdy(by cpu). Then driver could download fw. (For SW handshake)
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_H2C_PATH_RDY This bit is set to 1 to means that AXIDMA_CH0 is realdy(by cpu). Then driver could use H2C to handshake with cpu. (For SW handshake)
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_FWDL_EN DCPU Firmware download enable. (For SW handshake)
This bit is enabled by driver to notify DCPU need FW Patch. This bit is cleared by DCPU to notify driver that FWDL is done, and driver should check B_BE_DCPU_FWDL_STS.
1:Enable(Default),
0:Disable.
186008F4h R_BE_DCPU_BOOT_REASON
[31:3] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[2:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_BOOT_REASON DCPU Boot Reason:
0: boot reason is Pwr_On (initial value or updated by FW)
1: boot reason is WDT, and Leve2_rstb (WCPU, Platform and peripheral) is reset and released. (DCPU_RST will be auto released after PLATFORM_RST released). This value should be updated by WDT.
2: boot reason is LPS (updated by FW)
Else, it is unknown state.
186008F8h R_BE_DCPU_DBG_SYS_CFG
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_DCPU_DBG_SYS_CFG Debug system config
18600C00h R_BE_SEC_CTRL
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_SEC_FWDL_TIME Recording secure FWDL time, 0: in first time FWDL, 1: in second time FWDL
[22] R/W 0 B_BE_SEC_AUTH_DONE Recording secure authentication done supporting WoWlan scenario, 0: undone, 1: done
[21:19] R/W 000 B_BE_SEC_DCPU_IDMEM_SIZE_CONFIG 4 modes for IMEM and DMEM size (KB):
- 3b'000 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (192, 320, 288, 192)
- 3b'001 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (192, 320, 320, 160)
- 3b'010 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (192, 352, 288, 160)
- 3b'100 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (192, 288, 320, 192)
[18:16] R/W 000 B_BE_SEC_WCPU_IDMEM_SIZE_CONFIG 4 modes for IMEM and DMEM size (KB):
- 3b'000 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (576, 800, 800, 96)
- 3b'001 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (576, 832, 736, 128)
- 3b'010 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (576, 768, 800, 128)
- 3b'100 for (IMEM0, IMEM0, DMEM0, DMEM1) = (576, 768, 832, 96)
[15] R 0 B_BE_SEC_BT_SEC1_DIS autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.BT_SEC_DIS_1 2 bits, 0: enable, 1: disable
[14] R 0 B_BE_SEC_BT_SEC0_DIS autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.BT_SEC_DIS_0 2 bits, 0: enable, 1: disable
[13:5] R/W 0 RSVD Reserved for EFUSE.SEC_CTRL
[4] R/W 1 B_BE_SEC_UART_RX_EN autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.UART_RX_EN 2 bits, 0: disable, 1: enabe
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_SEC_UART_TX_EN autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.UART_TX_EN 2 bits, 0: disable, 1: enabe
[2] R/W 1 B_BE_SEC_JTAG_EN autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.JTAG_EN 2 bits, 0: disable, 1: enabe
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_SEC_SIC_EN autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.SIC_EN 2 bits, 0: disable, 1: enabe
[0] R 0 B_BE_SEC_SEC_DIS autoload value by OR result of EFUSE.SEC_DIS 2 bits, 0: enable, 1: disable
18600C04h R_BE_FILTER_MODEL_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0x18608000 B_BE_SEC_FILTER_MODEL_ADDR The base address for filter model
18600C30h R_BE_EFUSE_CTRL_S
[31:30] R/W 0x0 B_BE_EF_MODE_SEL_S Efuse controller Mode selection
2’b00 : Read
2’b10 : Write
others : no action
[29] R/W 0x0 B_BE_EF_RDY_S Ready bit
Note : HW set , SW clear
[28:16] R/W 0x0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_EF_ADDR_S Access Address
need 4 Byte Alignment (ex: 0,4,…..)
18600C34h R_BE_EFUSE_CTRL_1_V1_S
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_EF_DATA_S Access Data
access data is need 4 byte alignment
[31:0] : Read / Write
18600C38h R_BE_EFUSE_TEST_V1_S
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_EF_SCAN_SADR_S Scan start Address (unit in byte)
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_EF_SCAN_EADR_S EFuse Scan End address
18601000h R_BE_SDIO_TX_CTRL
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_SDIO_INT_TIMEOUT It is SDIO status timeout. The unit is 32usec. The default value is 0xc00 means that timeout is 98304 usec ( 32*0xc00)
Due to HW design, HW takes 0~3 as 0.
[15] R/W1C 0 B_BE_IO_ERR_STATUS If there is any io error due to Clock mismatch, set this flag.
Driver write 1 to clear it
[14] R 0 B_BE_CMD53_W_MIX This bit indicates the tx-packet is divided into two CMD53 (block + byte, byte + byte or block + block mode).

If B_BE_CMD53_TX_FORMAT = 0 or use CMD53 byte mode, this bit indicates CMD53 data length < Aggregation Len_4_BYTE_CNT
If B_BE_CMD53_TX_FORMAT = 1 and use CMD53 block mode, this bit indicates SEQ of a CMD53 Tx = SEQ of the previous CMD53 Tx.
[13] R/W 0 B_BE_CMD53_TX_FORMAT 0: Original format. Treats CMD53-address as data size (unit = 4 Byte)
1: Treats block mode CMD53-address as empty data size (unit = 4 Byte)
data length = block_cnt * block_length – address * 4 (address = dummy length. Unit = 4 byte)

If sets this bit = 1, HW will clear B_BE_CMD53_W_MIX to 0.
[12] R/W 0 B_BE_CMD53_R_TIMEOUT_MASK 1: mask CMD53 data timeout (for debug)
[11:10] R/W 0 B_BE_CMD53_R_TIMEOUT_UNIT Adjust the unit of cmd53 read timeout counter, T: SDIO_CLK
00 = 1T
01 = 2T
10 = 4T
11 = 8T
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_REPLY_ERRCRC_IN_DATA Default: 0
When this bit is set to 1 and there is a CMD53 read issue, fill in incorrect CRC value in the reply Data
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_En_CMD53_Overlap allow new coming cmd53 reset the unfinished cmd53 process
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_REPLY_ERR_IN_R5 Default: 0
When this bit is set to 1 and there is a data sync issue due to Clock mismatch, set ERROR flag(Bit3 and Bit0) in R5 response.
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_R18A_EN When this bit is auto load to 1, Device can accept host request for switch IO voltage to 1.8v
0:disable;
1: Set R18A in Response4		 0x310[6]
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_SDIO_CMD_FORCE_VLD When this bit is auto load to 1, Device can accept any command regardless of card’s current state
0:disable;
1:enable response for CMD in all card states		 0x310[5]
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_INIT_CMD_EN When this bit is set to 1, Device can accept CMD0, CMD3, CMD5 when bus is under command state 0x310[4]
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_RXINT_READ_MASK_DIS 0: RX_REQUEST interrupt will be masked after reading R_BE_SDIO_HISR.
1: RX_REQUEST interrupt will not be masked after reading R_BE_SDIO_HISR.
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_EN_RXDMA_MASK_INT If this bit set to 0, RX_REQUEST interrupt will raised immediately when RX data is ready.
If this bit set to 1 and status information is sent, RX_REQUEST interrupt will not raise. Only If INT_TIME_OUT is 1, interrupt will not be raised until timeout.
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_EN_MASK_TIMER The bit is only valid when EN_RXDMA_MASK_ INT is 1.
If this bit set to 0, timeout is disable.
If this bit set to 1, it enable interrupt timeout.
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMD_ERR_STOP_INT_EN If this bit set to 1, then SDIO receive illegal command or wrong CRC will stop send interrupt to host.
18601004h R_BE_SDIO_CTRL
[31:28] R/W 0x03 B_BE_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_D Driver Type D mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type D (CCCR 0x15[2] = 1)		 0x315[7:4]
[27:24] R/W 0x05 B_BE_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_C Driver Type C mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type C (CCCR 0x15[1] = 1)		 0x315[3:0]
[23:20] R/W 0x07 B_BE_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_B Driver Type B mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E		 0x314[7:4]
[19:16] R/W 0x0F B_BE_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_A Driver Type A mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type A (CCCR 0x15[0] = 1)		 0x314[3:0]
[15:9] R 0 RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_SIG_OUT_PH This bit is used to invert the SDCLK phase for SDR50 and SDR104 mode.
0 : Does not invert the SDCLK.
1 : To invert the SDCLK (push/pull signals 0.5T earlier).
Autoload from Efuse 0x123[6].		 0x313[0]
[7:4] R 0 B_BE_CMD11_SEQ_END_DELAY The time duration of device to push CMD/DAT lines high after receiving SD_CLK again.
Unit: 1-T SD_CLK.		 0x312[7:4]
[3:1] R 0 B_BE_CMD11_SEQ_SAMPLE_INTERVAL Sampling interval of ANA_CLK for detecting SD_CLK.
Unit: 64-T ANA_CLK.		 0x312[3:1]
[0] R 0 B_BE_CMD11_SEQ_EN If this bit set to 1, enable cmd11 signal voltage switch sequence function.
If this bit set to 0, only a R1 response will be send to respond to CMD11.		 0x312[0]
18601008h R_BE_SDIO_MONITOR
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23:0] R 0 B_BE_tsf_freerun_cnt_reg
1860100Ch R_BE_SDIO_MONITOR_2
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_CMD53_WT_EN 1: enable, 0: disable
If getting CMD53 write CMD, Start counting SDIO clock (from 0) when response end
Stop counting when CMD53 DATA start
The SDIO clock count is reported to B_BE_SDIO_CLK_CNT
Only can use CMD52 to write
[22:21] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_CLK_MONITOR 1: 200T MAC_CLK
2: 2000T MAC_CLK
3: 360000T MAC_CLK
Start counting SDIO clock (from 0) when this reg be set to non-zero value
Stop counting when timeout (200T, 2000T or 360000T) and reset this reg to 0
The SDIO clock count is reported to B_BE_SDIO_CLK_CNT
Write, polling 0 ready
Only can use CMD52 to write
[20:0] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_CLK_CNT SDIO clock count
18601010h R_BE_SDIO_CTRL_2
[31:2] R 0 RSVD
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_SDIO_CLK_SMT SD_CLK Schmitt trigger setting:
1: enable Schmitt trigger of SD_CLK, input Pad latch high/low follow SD SPEC.
0: disable Schmitt trigger of SD_CLK, input Pad latch high/low follow 50% signal level.
autoload from efuse 0x173[1]		 0x316[1]
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_SDIO_DATA_SMT SD_DATA/SD_CMD Schmitt trigger setting:
1: enable Schmitt trigger of SD_DATA/SD_CMD, input Pad latch high/low follow SD SPEC.
0: disable Schmitt trigger of SD_DATA/SD_CMD, input Pad latch high/low follow 50% signal level.
autoload from efuse 0x173[0]		 0x316[0]
18601014h R_BE_SDIO_MONITOR_3
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_mac_thr
18601018h R_BE_SDIO_LOCAL_REG
[31:22] RO 10'h0 RSVD
[21:14] RW 8'h0 B_BE_ioreg2apb_timeout_th
[13:12] RW 2'h0 B_BE__tunit_sel
[11] RW 1'b0 B_BE_fen_tunit
[10:9] RW 2'h0 B_BE_ioreg2apb_clr_mode
[8] RW 1'b0 B_BE_cmd_ioreg_hw_clr_en
[7:6] RW 1'b0 B_BE_rx_len_up_mode 在rx_async_FIFO read count大於等於rx_len_up_mode的情況下才會update rx_agg_length和發送rxdma_req。
2'b00: 0
2'b01: rx_fifo_depth/2
2'b10: rx_fifo_depth/4
2'b11: rx_fifo_depth
[5] RO 1'b0 RSVD
[4] RO 1'b0 B_BE__mac_is_active
[3] RW 1'b1 B_BE_sdio_ioreg_rst
[2] RW 1'b0 B_BE_disable_0x800
[1] RW 1'b0 B_BE_en_cmd53_rx_mix_mode
[0] RW 1'b0 B_BE_en_rx_len_cmd_ok
1860101Ch R_BE_RSVD0
[31:16] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
[15:0] RW 16'h0 B_BE_cmd11_timeout_threshold
18601020h R_BE_SDIO_DUMMY_REG_RW_1
[31:24] RW 8'h00 B_BE_sdio_dummy_reg_rw_1_31_24
[23:16] RW 8'h00 B_BE_sdio_dummy_reg_rw_1_23_16
[15:8] RW 8'h00 B_BE_sdio_dummy_reg_rw_1_15_8
[7:0] RW 8'h00 B_BE_sdio_dummy_reg_rw_1_7_0

18601030h R_BE_SDIO_HTSFR_INFO
[31:16] R 0 B_BE_HTSFT2
[15:0] R 0 B_BE_HTSFT1
18601034h R_BE_SDIO_TX_IOREG_INFO
[31:3] RO 30'h00000000 RSVD
[2] RW 1'b0 B_BE_ioreg_w_busy_en
[1] RW 1'b0 B_BE_tx_trigger_delay
[0] RO 1'b0 RSVD
18601038h R_BE_RSVD
[31:16] RO 16'h0 RSVD
[15:0] RO 16'h0 B_BE_hcpwm2_sdio_sync
003Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
18601040h R_BE_SDIO_INDIRECT_ADDR
[31] W1C 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_RDY Polling to 1, it indicates the indirect access R/W is ready.
Write 1 Clear.
[30:0] R/W 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_ADDR When access HPON register using high-speed sdio clock, data timeout may occur, so indirect access will be used. This register sets the indirect access Ioreg Addr bit
18601044h R_BE_SDIO_INDIRECT_DATA
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_DATA When INDIRECT_ACCESS_READY flag is set to 1, host can read this register for required data. Here the read data has been rotated if INDIRECT_ADDR[1:0] != 2’b00 (non-four byte alignment)
e.g., when INDIRECT_ADDR[1:0] = 2’b01, the four-byte read data is ORIG_RDATA[31:0], INDIRECT_RDATA = {ORIG_RDATA[7:0], ORIG_RDATA[31:8]}
18601048h R_BE_SDIO_INDIRECT_CTRL
[31:8] R 0 RSVD
[7:4] RO 4'h1 B_BE_indirect_ver ver = 0: 1115
ver = 1: 8922A/8952A
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_REG_R When host write this bit to 1, the indirect read request will be issued. This bit is always read as 0
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_REG_W When host write this bit to 1, the indirect write request will be issued. This bit is always read as 0
[1:0] R/W 0 B_BE_INDIRECT_REG_SIZE INDIRECT_SIZE=0: Access one byte
INDIRECT_SIZE=1: Access two bytes
INDIRECT_SIZE=2: Access four bytes
Only indirect writing need to set this field.
004Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0050h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0054h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0058h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
005Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0060h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0064h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0068h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
006Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
18601070h R_BE_SDIO_TRX_STAT
[31:15] RO 0 RSVD
[14:12] RO 0 B_BE_r_txdma_cs 0: idle
[11:8] RO 0 B_BE_r_txdata_cs 0: idle
[7:4] RO 0 B_BE_r_rxdma_cs 0: idle
[3:0] RO 0 B_BE_r_rxdata_cs 0: idle
0074h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
0078h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
007Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
18601080h R_BE_SDIO_HRPWM1_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_HRPWM
[15:0] R 0 RSVD
18601084h R_BE_SDIO_BUS_CTRL
[31:26] R 0 RSVD
[25] RW 1'b0 B_BE_local_reg_apb_en
[24] RW 1'b0 B_BE_rx_gclk_en 0x318[5]
[23] RW 1'b0 B_BE_tx_gclk_en 0x318[4]
[22] R 0 B_BE_r_cccr_ehs SDIO data output phase change immediately once CCCR offset 0x13 bus speed mode register value is rewritten
1: Rising edge , 0: Falling edge		 0x318[3]
[21] R 1 B_BE_ee_spi_clk_option 0x318[2]
[20] R 1 B_BE_INTR_CTRL This bit is written only by auto load, if load to 1, when device is parsing CMD, no interrupt will be issued during this time interval; otherwise, device can still issue interrupt to host even when CMD line is busy
[19] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_VOLTAGE This bit is written only by auto load, it indicates the card’s working voltage, i.e., 3.3v or 1.8v
[18] R 0 B_BE_BYPASS_INIT When this bit is auto load to 1, HW will by pass SDIO initial stage, and enter standby mode directly
[17] R 0 B_BE_HCI_RESUME_RDY 1: WL in Active state, all power and clock are enabled and driver may normally operate in active mode.
0: WL in suspend mode. WL clock power are turned off.
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_SUS_REQ 0 -> 1: driver indicates to suspend. SYSON will report “0” to HCI_SUS_STS in Suspend mode.
1 -> 0: driver to resume WL function via CMD52 only. SYSON will report “1” to indicate WL resume power and clock ready.
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_fn1_crc16_sync_en
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_cmd53_rdata_early 1: CMD53 read support data start before Response end
若開啟此 function, 0x18[0] 要一起設 1, 不然在 Rx block+byte mode 會回傳重複 data		 0x317[3]
[13] R/W 0 B_BE_HISR_W_CLR_EN When set to 1, HW will by pass HIMR check to do HISR write 1 clear. 0x317[2]
[12] R/W 0 B_BE_INT_MASK_DIS This bit is for sdio_clk >> ana_clk or mac_clk case. Its too late to wait sync ready and SD_D1 must leave interrupt state immediately after writing HIMR to 0.
0: Enable mask interrupt by sdio_clk domain.
1: Disable mask interrupt by sdio_clk domain.
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_PAD_CLK_XHGE_EN When host change bus speed mode, by writing CCCR related register, if this register is set to 1, output data sampling mode(posedge/negedge of SD CLK)will not changed until current CMD process is completed. Otherwise, data sampling mode will change immediately once CCCR register value is rewritten
[10] R/W 0 B_BE_INTER_CLK_EN When set to 1, SDIO CLK will be gated for power saving if no command nor data is received
[9] R/W 1 B_BE_EN_RPT_TXCRC When set to 1, device response negative ACK when received data has CRC errors
[8] R/W 1 B_BE_DIS_RXDMA_STS When set to 0, if device is under TXDMA mode, device will always response with positive ACK even if the received data has CRC error
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_hps_clkr
18601088h R_BE_SDIO_RESPONSE_TIMER
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23:16] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_CMD_CRC Display the calculated CRC value of the SDIO CMD which is considered CRC ERR
[15:0] R/W 0x0A B_BE_CMDIN_2RESP_TIMER This timer define the timeout interval between command and response, if this timeout interval is reached, the response will be forced to output
008Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
18601090h R_BE_SDIO_HSISR
[31:24] R/W 0 B_BE_sdio_resp_flag_en
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_sdio_resp_flag
[15:10] R 0 RSVD
[9] RW 1'h0 B_BE_hisr_mask_hs1
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_HISR_MASK SDIO Host system interrupt status mask , write 1 to enable, 0 to mask
[7:4] RO 6'h00 RSVD
[3] RW 1'b0 B_BE_hisr_w1_clr_hs1
[2] RO 1'b0 B_BE_drv_hs1_int
[1] RW 0 B_BE_DRV_WLAN_INT_CLR Write 1 to clear DRV_WLAN_INT
[0] R 0 B_BE_DRV_WLAN_INT Read only, interrupt status from HISR, mainly including power on , power off , SPS_OCP, GPIO interrupt
18601094h R_BE_SDIO_EXTEND_RBLOK_GAP
[31:6] R 0 RSVD
[5:0] R/W 0 B_BE_Extend_rblock_gap SDIO support adjustable Read Block Gap
SDR104/DDR50: (4+7+ Extend_rblock_gap) * SD_CLK
Others: : (4+3+ Extend_rblock_gap) * SD_CLK
0098h [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
009Ch [31:0] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
186010C0h R_BE_SDIO_DIOERR_RPT
[31:24] R 0 B_BE_DATA_CRC_ERR_CNT The number of CRC error of SDIO Data Block
[23:16] R 0 B_BE_CMD_CRC_ERR_CNT The number of CRC error of SDIO Command
[15:0] R 0 RSVD
186010C4h R_BE_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_L Cmd 53: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, block mode, OP code, register address, byte/block mode};
Cmd 52: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, raw flag, Stuff, register address, stuff, write data};
186010C8h R_BE_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT
[31:16] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_DATA_CRC Display the CRC value of the SDIO DATA which is considered CRC ERR
[15] R 0 B_BE_FN1_WDATA_TO_FLG CMD53 write FN1, 若沒收到write data, 則
當收到另一個CMD52/CMD53 read/write FN1 register,
觸發timeout event, 設B_BE_WDATA_TO_FLG = 1

Note: 若另一個CMD52是直接read B_BE_WDATA_TO_FLG, 可能無法當下反應此timeout event至R_data, 需要再read一次
[14] RO 1'b0 B_BE_fn1_less_wdata_flg CMD53 write FN1, 若有收到write data但length尚不足, 則
當收到另一個CMD52/CMD53 read/write FN1 register,
觸發timeout event, 設B_BE_WDATA_LEN_SHORT_FLG = 1

Note: 若另一個CMD52是直接read B_BE_WDATA_LEN_SHORT_FLG, 可能無法當下反應此timeout event至R_data, 需要再read一次
[13] RO 1'b0 RSVD

This bit is set when LR.AVAL _IND INT is issued due to available page is greater than threshold and AVAL_IND_EN is set to 1.
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_REQUEST_INT_EN This bit is set when one complete RX aggregation packet is received into RXPKTBUF and RX_REQUEST _EN is set to 1.
Only when RXPKTBUF is empty, the bit becomes zero.

[1] R 0 B_BE_SDIO_AVAL_INT HCI channel available page Indication Interrupt:
This bit is set when LR.AVAL _IND INT is issued due to available page is greater than threshold and B_BE_SDIO_AVAL_INT_EN is set to 1.
Only when all of B_BE_ACH_INTRPT_STAT are 0, the bit becomes zero.
Note: also support W1C
[0] R 0 B_BE_RX_REQUEST_INT This bit is set when one complete RX aggregation packet is received into RXPKTBUF and B_BE_RX_REQUEST_INT_EN is set to 1.
Only when RXPKTBUF is empty, the bit becomes zero.
Note: also support W1C

18601808h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_208
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_11
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_10

18601808h R_BE_GMAC_MAR0
[31:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR0 Multicast Register 0, The MAR register0-7 are only permitted to write by 4-byte access. Read access can be byte, word, or double word access. Driver is responsible for initializing these registers. The MAR7-0 define 64 bits which is a bit wise index of the multicast function of multicast addresses. The hash function of multicast address is the upper 6 MSB’s of the CRC32 of the address (destination). The index then is the numerical representation of those 6 bits in hex format.
[23:16] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR1 Multicast Register 1
[15:8] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR2 Multicast Register 2
[7:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR3 Multicast Register 3
1860180Ch R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_20C

[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_12
1860180Ch R_BE_GMAC_MAR1
[31:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR4 Multicast Register 4
[23:16] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR5 Multicast Register 5
[15:8] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR6 Multicast Register 6
[7:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_MAR7 Multicast Register 7
18601810h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_210
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_19

18601810h R_BE_GMAC_TxOkCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TxOK 16-bit counter of Tx Ok packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxOK 16-bit counter of Rx Ok packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
18601814h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_214
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_23
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_22
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_21
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_20
18601814h R_BE_GMAC_TxErrCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TxErr 16-bit counter of TxErr packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. The source of TxErr are txunder, txabt(in half-duplex mode) or txlinkerr.
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxErr 16-bit counter of RxErr packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
The source of RxErr are GMAC meets the following condition when rx fifo is not full.:
1. RCR.accept runt = 0, but the ingress pkt length < 64B.
2. RCR.accepterr = 0, but the ingress pkt is a CRC error pkt.
18601818h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_218
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_27
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_26
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_25
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_24
18601818h R_BE_GMAC_MissPktCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_MissPkt 16-bit counter of Missed packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_FAE 16-bit counter of FAE (Frame Alignment Error) packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. RCR.AER should be high.
When FAE occurs in 10/100mode, the following thins happens:
1. Rx status des.CRC error does not assert if the ingress(exclude alignment error nibble) if correct.
2. RxErr_reg increased by 1.
3. FAE_reg increased by 1
4. Rx status des.RCDF does not asserts.
5. data length does not include alignment error nibble.
1860181Ch R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_21C
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_31
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_30
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_29
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_28
1860181Ch R_BE_GMAC_Tx1ColCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_Tx1Col 16-bit counter of TxCol packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. This only record which have entered just one collision before Tx OK
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TxMCol 16-bit counter of TxCol packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. This keeps track of those packets with less than 16 collisions (or the configured retry count) before Tx Ok
18601820h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_220
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_35
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_34
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_33
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_32
18601820h R_BE_GMAC_RxOkCNT
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxOkPhy 16-bit counter of Rx Ok packets with physical address matching destination address. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxOkBrd 16-bit counter of Rx Ok packets with broadcast destination address.. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
18601824h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_224
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_39
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_38
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_37
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_36
18601824h R_BE_GMAC_RxOkMulCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxOkMul 16-bit counter of Rx Ok packets with multicast destination address. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear.
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxOkAbt 16-bit counter of Tx aborted packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. This accounts for over collision, underrun, LNK failure conditions.
18601828h R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_228
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_43
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_42
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_41
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_40
18601828h R_BE_GMAC_TxUnderCNT
[31:16] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TXUNDERC 16-bit counter of Tx Underrun packets. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. GMAC only.
[15:0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU_MISSPKTC 16-bit counter of miss packets due to rx descriptor is unavailable. Rolls over automatically. Write to clear. GMAC only.
1860182Ch R_BE_SDIO_FN2_CIS_22C
[31:24] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_47
[23:16] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_46
[15:8] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_45
[7:0] RO 8'h0 B_BE_fn2_cis_44
1860182Ch R_BE_GMAC_Dummy_2C
[31:0] RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_2C
18601830h R_BE_GMAC_Dummy_30
[31:0 RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_30
18601834h R_BE_GMAC_TRXR
[31:3] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[2] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_TXUNDER Transmit FIFO Underrun: Set to 1 if the Tx FIFO was exhausted during the transmission of a packet.
[1:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
18601838h R_BE_GMAC_COM
[31:7] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[6] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_Counter_Rst Reset counters. Write 1 to trigger reset, when reset is done, auto clear to 0.
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Tdsc_Debug Tx descriptor contains debug message.
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Disable_Lso_Err Disable lso err condition.
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxJumbo 1: support jumbo packet receiving.
The maximum packet size is 16384B(0x4000) 16379B.
0: Does not support jumbo packet receiving.

When rxjumbo sets to 1, the minium rx descriptor ring size is 16.
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxVLAN Receive VLAN de-tagging enable. 1: Enable. 0: Disable.
1: detag vlan and set rx status dsc.tava = 1 when ingress packet has vlantag.
0: disable detag vlan and rx status dsc.tava = 0.
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RxChkSum Receive checksum offload enable. 1: Enable. 0: Disable.
[0] R/SC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RST Reset: Setting to 1 forces the Ethernet module to a software reset state which disables the transmitter and receiver, reinitializes the FIFOs, triggers interrupt Swint for RISC to reset the system buffer pointer to the initial value Tx/Rx FDP. The values of IDR0-5 and MAR0-7 will have no changes.

This bit is 1 during the reset operation, and is cleared to 0 by the Ethernet Module when the reset operation is complete.
1860183Ch R_BE_GMAC_IMSR
[31] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU6_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[30] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU5_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[29] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU4_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[28] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU3_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[27] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU2_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[26] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_SWInt_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[25] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_TDU_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_LinkChg_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[23] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_TER_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[22] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_TOK_TI_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[21] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[20] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_PER_OVF_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[19] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_Hw_Txu1_Err_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[18] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_PER_RUNT_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[17] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_CNT_WRAP_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[16] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_ROK_En 1: enable interrupt
0: disable interrupt
[15] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU6 Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING6:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[14] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU5 Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING5:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[13] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU4 Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING4:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[12] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU3 Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING3:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[11] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU2 Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING2:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[10] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_SWInt Software Interrupt pending:
When set to 1 indicates a software interrupt was forced. Write 1 to clear.
[9] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TDU Tx Descriptor Unavailable:
When set, indicates Tx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[8] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_LinkChg Link Change:
Write 1 to clear.
In rle0639, gmac is used as cpu port of switch, default is 1’b1.
[7] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TER Transmit (Tx) Error:
Indicates that a packet transmission was aborted, due to excessive collisions, according to the TXRR's setting. Write 1 to clear.
[6] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_TOK_TI Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
Transmit (Tx) Error:
Indicates that a packet transmission was aborted, due to excessive collisions, according to the TXRR's setting. Write 1 to clear.
[5] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_RDU Rx Descriptor Unavailable for RING1:
When set, indicates Rx descriptor is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[4] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_PER_OVF Rx FIFO Overflow, caused by RBO/RDU, poor system bus (Lexra bus) performance, or overloaded Lexra bus traffic.
Write 1 to clear.
[3] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_Hw_txu1_err Tx module occur lso_err
[2] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_PER_RUNT Rx error caused by runt error characterized by the frame length in bytes being less than 64 bytes.Write 1 to clear.
[1] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_CNT_WRAP Tx/rx counter wrap interrupt.
[0] R/WC 0 B_BE_GMAC_ROK Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires.Write 1 to clear.
18601840h R_BE_GMAC_TCR
[31:20] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[19:16] R/W 0x6 B_BE_GMAC_Preamble_Sel TX preamble config :
preamble_cfg[3:0] , range= 0~15. (default=6)
final preamble= 2 byte + preamble_cfg[3:0] ,
GMAC default TX preamble=8 byte
(the final preamble range=2~17 byte)
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Tx_Jumbo 0: Does not support tx jumbo frame
1: support tx jumbo frame(max: 9KB).

gmac 若是打 tx jumbo pkt, 則無法做 tx checksum offload.
因為, gmac 內 tx fifo 只有 2KB. 若是打 tx jumbo pkt,
硬體沒辦法看到完整的 tx data payload, 因此, 算不出 checksum value .
rle0676 gmac 使用上, tx jumbo and tx cs offload 無法同時支援.若是同時打開的話, gmac 會 hang 住.

Info of tx jumbo pkt (20170209):
1. no checksum offload.
2. iocmd.earlytx = 1.
3. txunder may occurs.
4. txunder issue 由 CONFIG_REG. r_en_tx_extra 來確保 txuder 不會發生.
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Txcs_Iplen IPv4 only.
0: In tx cs offload, using tx_cmd_dsc.data_len only.
1: In tx cs offload, checks with ip total length field.

This bit is valid in single tx cmd dsc operation or hw padding for runt pkt (RLE0947).
[13] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[12:10] R/W 0x4 B_BE_GMAC_IFG_Sel InterFrameGap Time: This field allows the user to adjust the interframe gap time IFG: TX IPG gap selection
IFG_sel [2:0] , range= 0~7. Byte

GMAC spec re-define as:
final TX_IPG= 8 byte + IFG [2:0] ,
GMAC default TXIPG=12 byte
(the final Tx IPG range will = 8~15 byte)
[9:8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_LBK The loopback function must be independent of the link state.
2’b00 : normal operation
2’b01 : Loopback mode_R2T(lbkmode_r2t) :
Lbk methld: no SW requirement.
1. receive ingress data.
2. assign rxd to txd (txd).
3. egress pkt.
2’b10 : Reserved
2’b11 : Loopback mode(lbk_mode).
Lbk method: 1. rx fet descriptor.
2. tx fet descriptor and data
3. put data in txfifo
4. assign txd to rxd.(lbk)
5. gmac mv data to lxb.
In this mode(lbk_mode), the recommended value of RC_REG(0x44) is 0xf.
[7:2] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Tx_Multipkt_Cnt_Pause 0: exclude pause frame in ifOutMulticastPkts_H/L
1: include pause frame in ifOutMulticastPkts_H/L
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Tx_Nopadding 1: HW does not pad short packet(<60B). For intact vlantag operation, SW chould set tx cmd dsc.vlan_action to 2’b00.
0: HW pads short packet to 64B.
18601844h R_BE_GMAC_RCR
[31:15] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[14:11] R/W 0x3 B_BE_GMAC_Frag_Timeout If the next fragment is not received over a time threshold, regard it as lost.
Time threshold = (Frag_Timeout+1) * 512 us
[10] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_TCPCSErr Control bit for determining the acceptance of RX packets with TCP checksum error
1: Packets with TCP checksum error will be received
0: Packets with TCP checksum error will be dropped
Note: Only valid under GMAC mode
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_UDPCSErr Control bit for determining the acceptance of RX packets with UDP checksum error
1: Packets with UDP checksum error will be received
0: Packets with UDP checksum error will be dropped
Note: Only valid under GMAC mode
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_IPCSErr Control bit for determining the acceptance of RX packets with IP checksum error
1: Packets with IP checksum error will be received
0: Packets with IP checksum error will be dropped
Note: Only valid under GMAC mode
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HomePNA Support home PNA mode.
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_AFlow Accept flow control : When set to 1, flow control packet will also be received & DMA to rx buffer for debug. Default is 0.
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_CERErr Control bit for determining the acceptance of RX packets with CRC error
When set to 1, all packets with CRC error, alignment error, and/or collided fragments will be accepted.
Rxerrc keeps,
Rxokc +1,
Rx status dsc.CRC =1,
Rx status dsc.RCDF =0.
When set to 0, all packets with CRC error, alignment error, and/or collided fragments will be rejected.
Rxerrc +1,
Rxokc keeps,
Rx status dsc.CRC =0,
Rx status dsc.RCDF =1.
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_Runt Control bit for determining the acceptance of runt packets (packet length < 64B)
This bit allows the receiver to accept packets that are smaller than 64 bytes. The packet length must be larger than 8 11 bytes to be accepted as a runt packet. Set to 1 to accept runt packets.
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_Broadcast Set to 1 to accept broadcast packets, 0 to reject.
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Accept_Multicast Set to 1 to accept multicast packets, 0 to reject.
RC_REG.AB and AM should be enabled simulatanously.
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_APM Set to 1 to accept physical match packets, 0 to reject.
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_AAP Set to 1 to accept all packets with physical destination address, 0 to reject.
18601848h R_BE_GMAC_CPUTAG0
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CTEN_RX Enable parsing ingress packet with cputag format.
[30:27] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CT_TSIZE Cputag size in egress pkt.
CT_Tsize: 4’h0: 0 Byte
4’h1: 4 Byte
4’h2: 8Byte
4’h3: 12 Byte
[26:25] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CT_RSIZE_H After rl8681 (including), this field is only for ingress pkt.
CTR_size: 4’h0: 0 Byte
4’h1: 4 Byte
4’h2: 8Byte
For Apollo cputag with PTP timestamp, set CT_RSIZE to 4’h2.
[24] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_CT_DSLRN In rtl8370s, Setting of Disable Learning field in tx cputag.
Setting of Disable Learning field in RTL8368 or DSLRN field in RTL8307h tx cputag when short_dsc_format = 0.
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CT_NORMK Setting of NORMK field in RTL8307h tx cputag when short_dsc_format = 0.
[22] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_CT_ASPRI In RTL8307h, setting ASPRI field in tx cputag when short_dsc_format = 0.
In RTL8370S, setting priority select field in tx cputag when short_dsc_format =0..
[21:18] R/W 0x8 B_BE_GMAC_CT_SWITCH Support cputag format of switch
0: no cputag support
1: 8368
2: 8306
3: 8307
4: 8370
5: gmac in 8681.
6: gmac in Apollo,
rle0371, rl6266 and
rtl9601(rle0639)
7. gmac in rle0676(rtl9602bvb)
8. gmac in rle0787
[17:16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CT_RSIZE_L
[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CTPM CPU tag protocol mask.
8306:0xf0
8368:0xe0
8370:0xff
8307h:0xff
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_CTPV CPU tag protocol value.
8306:0x90
8368:0xa0 or 0xb0
8370:0x04
8307h:0x04
1860184Ch R_BE_GMAC_CONFIG
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Lxm64 0: mst_gmac_bad[31:0]
1: mst_gmac_bad[63:0]
[30] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[29:28] R 0x2 B_BE_GMAC_Rff_Size_Sel Set gmac_rxfifo size.
2’b00: 1KB (remove @ 20160726)
2’b01: 1664B
2’b10: 2KB
[27] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Tso_ID_Sel Uses to choose LSO(TCP) IP.identification value
0: keep.
1: incremental.
[26] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[25] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_En_Int_Route 0: disable interrupt routing, IMR0_reg, IMR1_reg.
1: enable interrupt routing, IMR0_reg, IMR1_reg.
[24] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_En_Int_Split When, en_int_route = 0,
0: only int.tdu, tok and rok present.
1: IMR0_reg, IMR1_reg and ISR1_reg present.
When en_int_route = 1, this field is 1.
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_En_RFF_Afull Enable rff almost full indication ability.
R_en_rff_afull = 1: 收到 rx jumbo and 發生 rdu. HW 不丟包
R_en_rff_afull = 0: 收到 rx jumbo and 發生 rdu. HW 丟包
接 PCS 的應用不適用.
[22] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Rxdv_Extra Set CONFIG_reg.r_en_rff_afull = 1.
When rff almost full occurs, gmac will assert gmii_rff_afull to switch.
When rff almost empty, gmac will de-assert gmii_rff_afull to switch.
No r2t loopback operation in this mode.
Gmii_rff_afull is PHY I/F side band signal.
[21] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_Hw_Flowctrl Set CONFIG_reg.r_en_rff_afull = 1.
When rff almost full occurs, gmac will transmit pause on packet.
When rff has enough space for the next packet, gamc will transmit pause off packet.
[20:17] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_En_Tx_Extra Support PHY I/F side band signal (gmii_txe_extra_#).
0: gmii_txe_extra_# is constant 1.
1: When txunder occurs, gmac will deasert gmii_txe_extra_# indicates txd is not valid.
[15:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
18601850h R_BE_GMAC_CPUTAG1
[31:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_r_reason_fb
[15] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[14:8] R/W 0x40 B_BE_GMAC_r_streamid For egress packet, when CPUTAG1_REG.r_stream_id equals to cputag.pon_stream_id, hw will not perform padding.

For ingress packet, when CPUTAG1_REG.r_stream_id equals to RTL9601_CPUTAG_RX_TYPE1.pon_stream_id or RTL9602BVB_CPUTAG_RX_TYPE1.pon_stream_id, hw will not detag vlantag.
In RLE0371/RL6266, default value is 7’h7f.
In RLE0639, default values is 7’h20
In RLE0676(RTL9602bvb) default value is 7’h40
[7:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
18601854h R_BE_GMAC_MICR
[31:7] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[6] RW 0x1 B_BE_GMAC_dis_dly_mode Disable rxc delay mode.
0: enable delay chain.
1: disable delay chain.

SW should change rxc_dly_sel after set dis_dly_mode to 1.
[5] RW 0x0 B_BE_GMAC_Set_d_txc Add delay to rgmii_txc_out. Used in clk250M.(?)
0= the same fhase as TXD.
1= add TXC delay compared to TXD.
Rgmii_1000: add 2nS delay.
Rgmii_100: add 4nS delay.
Rgmii_10: add 4nS delay.
[4:0] RW 0x0 B_BE_GMAC_Rxc_dly_sel 32-tap delay chain for rxc.
Add internal input delay to RXC to implement the RXD setup time and hold time required spec at the input side.

This field is valid when CONFIG_CLK_REG.dis_dly_mode is 0.
00000 = 0 ns.
00001 = 0.2ns
00010 = 0.4ns
00011 = 0.6ns
...
00111 = 1.4ns
…
10000 =3.2ns
10001 = 3.4ns
10010 = 3.6ns
10011 = 3.8ns
18601858h R_BE_GMAC_MSR
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Force_TRXFCE Force Tx/RX Flow Control:
1 = enabled Flow control in the absence of NWAY.
0 = disables Flow control in the absence of NWAY.
Version effect: rl6166(ECO) and after.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RXFCE RX Flow control Enable: The flow control is enabled in full-duplex mode only. Packets are dropped if buffer is exhausted. Default is 0.
1 = Rx Flow Control Enabled.
0 = Rx Flow Control Disabled.
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_TXFCE Tx Flow Control Enable:
1 = tx flow control enabled.
0 = tx flow control disabled.
ACCEPT ERROR MUST NOT BE ENABLED
[28] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_SPEED_1000 {SPEED_1000, SPEED_10} 10: 1000Mbps, 11: not allowed
[27] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_SPEED_10 {SPEED_1000, SPEED_10} 00: 100Mbps, 01: 10Mbps
[26] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_LINKB Inverse of Link status. 0 = Link OK. 1 = Link Fail.
[25] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_TXPF Tx Pause frame: 1 = Ethernet module has sent a pause packet. 0 = the Ethernet module has sent a timer done packet.
[24] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_RXPF Rx Pause frame: 1 = Ethernet module has receive a pause packet. 0 = the Ethernet module has sent a timer done packet.
[23] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_SEL_RGMII gmac_sel_rgmii
[22] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_FULLDUPREG Indicates Full duplex mode from mdio results.
[21] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_NWCOMPLETE Nway complete
[20] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_SEL_MII indicates in mii mode.
[19] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_FORCEDFULLDUP force gmac operates at full duplex mode.
This bit is Write only in RLE0315.
This bit is R/W in RLE0390 (原RL6166).
1’b1: force gmac in full duplex mode.
1’b0: duplex status is from MDIO auto-polling. Not means gmac is in half duplex mode.
[18] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_FORCELINK force gmac in link ok mode.
This bit is Write only in RLE0315.
This bit is R/W in RLE0390 (RL6166).
1’b1: force gmac in linkok.
1’b0: link status is from MDIO auto-polling.
[17:16] R/W 0x3 B_BE_GMAC_FORCE_SPD Force gmac in 10/100/GIGA mode.
2’b00: 100M
2’b01: 10M
2’b10: GIGA
2’b11: not force mode.
These bits are Write only in RLE0390 (RL6166).
The current speed status of gmac can be checked in MSR_reg.{speed1000, speed_10}.

In force mode, SW can set gmac operates in 100M, FD and Link ok(for example) and SW can not set gmac operates in 100M, HD and Link ok. When FORCEDFULLDUP is 1’b0, the duplex status is from MDIO auto-polling, therefore, it is not controlled by SW.
[15] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_gmac_sel_phyif_0 1: phy interface 0.works.
0: phy interface 1 works.
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_PFC_EN Config flow control pause frame type
0: normal pause frame (opcode=0x0001)
1: priority flow control (opcode=0x0101)
[13] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_gmac_phy_mode 1: in phy mode.
0: not in phy mode.
[12] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_rgmii_rx_decode_sts 0: Does not support rgmii in band status(link status, speed and duplex mode of the PHY) by decoding rxd.
1: Supports rgmii in band status(link status, speed and duplex mode of the PHY) by decoding rxd.
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_rgmii_tx_encode_sts This field is valid only in phy mode.

0: Does not support rgmii in band status(link status, speed and duplex mode of the PHY) by encoding txd.
1: Supports rgmii in band status(link status, speed and duplex mode of the PHY) by encoding txd.
[10] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_force_spd_mode 1: gmac is in force speed mode. The real speed in force mode is set in MS_REG.FORCE_SPD.
0: gmac speed status is from md operation.
[9:8] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_Eee_aby Indicates eee ability of PCS.
Eee_aby[0]: eee ability in 100M
Eee_aby[1]: eee ability in giga mode.
[7:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
1860185Ch R_BE_GMAC_MIIAR
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Flag Flag bit, used to identify access to MII register:
1: Write data to MII register. Turns to 0 automatically upon completion of MAC writing to the specified MII register.
0: Read data from MII register. Turns to 1 automatically upon completion of MAC reading the specified MII register.
Read write turn around time I s about 64 us.
[30:26] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_PHYAddress Defines the Phy address for the MII.
[25:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_En_eeep_auto_write HW auto writes PCS.page4.reg20 and reg27 to adjust analog parameters based on eeep_lpi state.

SW should change page to page4 then set MIIA_REG.en_eeep_auto_write. HW will write eeep_abiq_on/off and eeep_ldvbias_on/off (in EEEP_CR6_REG and EEEP_CR7_REG) to PCS.page4.reg20 and reg27, respectively, based on eeep_lpi state.

If SW wants to access another page of PCS, SW should disable MIIA_REG.en_eeep_auto_write.
[22] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Disable auto polling Disable auto polling feature of mdio operation.
0: HW auto polling PCS status.
1: HW does not auto polling PCS status.
[21] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_polling_eee_reg polling PCS EEE advertisement register (7.60). In rl6166(原rle0315) and before: this bit is not supported.
In rle0390: In in-direct method, gmac had to access PCS register by multiples R/W of reg13 and 14. It is OK for PCS to be aborted in the middle of indirect method.
In rle0371: HW will complete current indirect access to PCS then stop in-direct method.

Please refer to EEE_CR1_reg.
[20:16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RegAddr Defines 5-bit MII register address.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Data Defines 16 bit MII resgister data.
Write when MIIA_REG.flag =1.
18601860h R_BE_GMAC_SWINT
[31:25] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[24] W/SC 0 B_BE_GMAC_SWINT_Trigger Write 1 will force ISR register bit 10 set to 1
[23:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
18601864h R_BE_GMAC_VLAN_REG
[31:16] R/W 0x88a8 B_BE_GMAC_STAG_PID Set the s-tag protocol identifier.
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_en_rstag Gmac rx.
0. disable STAG_PID.
1: enable STAG_PID.
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_en_tstag Gmac tx.
0. disable STAG_PID.
1: enable STAG_PID.
[13:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
18601868h R_BE_GMAC_VLAN_REG1
[31:16] R/W 0x88a8 B_BE_GMAC_STAG_PID Set the s-tag protocol identifier. This field is valid only when COM_DSC.TDSC_VLAN_TYPE and VLAN1_REG. r_en_rstag1/r_en_tstag1 is high.
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_en_rstag1 Gmac rx.
0. disable STAG_PID1.
1: enable STAG_PID1.
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_en_tstag1 Gmac tx.
0. disable STAG_PID1.
1: enable STAG_PID1.
[13:0] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
1860186Ch R_BE_GMAC_Dummy_6C
[31:0] RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_6C
18601870h R_BE_GMAC_LED_CTRL
[31:20] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[19] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_eee_en_led Enable/disable lpi led.
[18] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Custom_led Custom led mode.
1: using custom led matrix table.
0: using Non-custom led table.
[17:16] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ledsel Non custom led select.
Following EEE spec, combine the Link signal and LPI signal in the same LED pin. While entering LPI state, LED will blink with 2400 2784ms cycle, of which the first 2000 2349ms LED light is turned off, after 400 435ms is turned on.

NO EEE in RL6166.
[15:12] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Ledse3 Custom led3 select.
[11:8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Ledse2 Custom led2 select.
[7:4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Ledse1 Custom led1 select.
[3:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Ledse0 Custom led0 select.
18601874h R_BE_GMAC_ETHP_CTRL
[31] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_TX_EN 1: Enable GMAC TX, 0: Disable GMAC TX
[30] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_RX_EN 1: Enable GMAC RX, 0: Disable GMAC RX
[29] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_TX_IDLE Assert when TX_EN is deasserted and no packet is transmitting
[28] R 1 B_BE_GMAC_RX_IDLE Assert when RX_EN is deasserted and no packet is receiving
[27:8] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP1_EN
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP1_Proto_EN
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP1_Desc_EN
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP0_EN
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP0_Proto_EN
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_ETHP0_Desc_EN
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
18601878h R_BE_GMAC_IO_CTRL
[31] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[30:16] R/W 0x7d0 B_BE_GMAC_Host_MRU Maximum receive unit (MRU) of the host SoC, including ETHP and 802.3 CRC.
- Unit: Byte
- Default value = 2000B, which means MAC of host SoC can receive packet of
1554B = 1536 (Wi-Fi Packet) + 14B (ETHP) + 4B (802.3 CRC)
[15:10] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Interrupt_Mode 0: Interrupt by GPIO
1: Interrupt by packet
[8:6] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_FRAG_EN Enable bit of Egress Frame Handler fragmentation
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD
[3:2] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RXFTH RX FIFO Theshold.
Specifies the threshold level in the Rx FIFO to begin the transmission. When the byte count of the data in the RX FIFO reaches this level, (or the FIFO contains at least one complete packet or the end of a packet) the RXDMA_Ctrl will transmit this packet.
00: 128B
01: 256B
10: 512B
11: 1024B
[1:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_TXFTH TX FIFO Theshold.
Specifies the threshold level in the Tx FIFO to begin the transmission. When the byte count of the data in the Tx FIFO reaches this level, (or the FIFO contains at least one complete packet or the end of a packet) the TXDMA_Ctrl will transmit this packet.
00: 128B
01: 256B
10: 512B
11: 1024B
1860187Ch R_BE_GMAC_Dummy_7C
[31:0] RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_7C
18601880h R_BE_GMAC_MEM_BIST
[31] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_DRF_BIST_DONE rx/tx fifo drf bist done.
[30] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_BIST_DONE rx/tx fifo bist done.
[29] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_DRF_RFF_fail Rx fifo drf bist fail.
[28] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_DRF_TFF_fail Tx fifo drf bist fail.
[27] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_RFF_fail RX fifo bist fail.
[26] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_TFF_fail TX fifo bist fail.
[25:12] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Csr_rff_dvse
[10:8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Csr_rff_dvs
[7:4] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Csr_tff_dvse
[2:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Csr_tff_dvs
18601884h R_BE_GMAC_PktGen
[31:16] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_en_pglbk Enable packet generator mode
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_pglbk_data_pat_sel 1: fixed pattern, 0: pseudo random
[13] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_pglbk_done packet generator in wire lbk mode status. 1: done
[12] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_pglbk_fail packet generator in wire lbk mode status. 1: fail
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_pblbk_pkt_length_sel 0: random (range: 64-1504B), 1: 64B
[10:9] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_pglbk_pkt_num_sel 2’b00: 100 pkts,
2’b10: 5 pkts.
2’b01: 1 pkt
2’b11: reserved.
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_R_disable_txcrc 1: not append CRC for egress packet.
0: append CRC for egress packet.
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_pglbk_data_pat Pattern inside tx packet when pglbk_data_pat_sel is high.
For RLE0516, only pglbk_data_pat[3:0] are used.
18601888h R_BE_GMAC_EEE_CR1
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EN_FRC_EEE Enable EEE force mode
0: Disable EEE FORCE Mode
1: Enable EEE FORCE Mode
If EN_FRC_EEE=1, both FRC_EEE_Giga and FRC_EEE_100 are used to force MAC operation ability.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_FRC_EEE_Giga If EN_FRC_EEE=1, FRC_EEE_Giga:
0: Disable MAC EEE ability for 1000BASE-T.
1: Enable MAC EEE ability for 1000BASE-T.
If EN_FRC_EEE=0, FRC_EEE_Giga means EEE advertisement register 7.60 bit[2]
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_FRC_EEE_100M If EN_FRC_EEE=1, FRC_EEE_100
0: Disable MAC EEE ability for 100BASE-TX.
1: Enable MAC EEE ability for 100BASE-TX.
If EN_FRC_EEE=0, FRC_EEE_100 means EEE advertisement register 7.60 bit[1]
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EN_EEE_10M Set same as PHY setting, for LED.
[27] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EN_EEE_TX Enable MAC TX EEE ability
0: Disable EEE ability for TX.
1: Enable EEE ability for TX.
[26] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EN_EEE_RX Enable MAC RX EEE ability
0: Disable EEE ability for RX.
1: Enable EEE ability for RX.
Application note : The following methods could be used to disable EEE function.
a) n[1:0] = 00 -> completely disable EEE function, or
b) n[3:2] = 00 -> disable 100M/1000Mbps EEE capability (excludes 10Mbps ).
[25:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_Eee_outq_lowq_over SW has many packets to be tranmitted, then set this bit to tell HW leave lpi state in condition2.
[22:20] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[19] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_STS Indicate EEE status, both TX and RX.
1: Both TX and RX in LPI mode simultaneously.
0: not both TX and RX in LPI mode simultaneously
[18] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_TX_STS Indicate EEE TX status
1: TX is in LPI mode.
0: TX is in normal mode.
[17] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_RX_STS Indicate Port 0 EEE RX status
1: RX is in LPI mode.
0: RX is in normal mode.
[16] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_PauseFlag Set indication for LPI request due to RX pause on frame.
1: indicate RX pause on frame;
0: no indicate.
[15] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Wake_Set1 Enable LPI TX high priority pkt Wakeup decision maker for EEE.
1: enable;
0: disable.
Gmac will leave lpi mode when EEE_CR1_reg.txq_th is met or one of the egress is larger than 512B.
[13] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Wake_Set0 Enable LPI TX transmit wakeup decision maker for EEE.
1: enable;
0: disable.
[12] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Req_Set2 Enable LPI TX request RX pause on frame decision maker for EEE.
1: enable;
0: disable.
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Req_Set1 Enable LPI TX request queue empty decision maker for EEE.
1: enable;
0: disable.
For RLE0390(原RL6166), only for tx is single descriptor operation.
For next version, this bit can support multi-descriptor operation.
[10] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Req_Set0 Enable LPI TX request Rate decision maker for EEE.
1: enable;
0: disable.
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_RXTimer_EN Enable RX meter to count RX LPI status time
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_TXTimer_EN Enable TX meter to count TX LPI status time
[7:6] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_1 Set the unit for timer T_wakeup, T_tx_rate, and T_pause in EEE function.
00: 1uS;
01: 16 uS;
10: 128 uS;
11: 1024uS.
[5:4] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_2 Set the unit for timer T_tx_delay in EEE function.
00: 8uS;
01: 64uS;
10: 256uS;
11: 1024uS.
[3:2] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Unit_100_1 Set the unit for timer T_wakeup, T_tx_rate, and T_pause in EEE function.
00: 1uS;
01: 16uS;
10: 128uS;
11: 1024uS.
[1:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Unit_100_2 Set the unit for timer T_tx_delay in EEE function.
00: 8uS;
01: 64uS;
10: 512uS;
11: 1024uS.
1860188Ch R_BE_GMAC_EEE_CR2
[31:24] R/W 0x10 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tw_Giga Timer value in EEE function for T_wakeup.
Value from 0~256.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_1.
Default value is 16 us.
[23:16] R/W 0xa B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tr_Giga Timer value in EEE function for T_tx_rate.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_1.
Default value is 10 us.
[15:8] R/W 0xc4 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Td_Giga Timer value in EEE function for QoS based LPI decision maker T_tx_delay.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_2*4.
Default value is 200 ms.
[7:0] R/W 0xa B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tp_Giga Timer value in EEE function for TX decision maker T_pause.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_Giga_1.
Default value is 10 us.
18601890h R_BE_GMAC_EEE_CR3
[31:24] R/W 0x1e B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tw_100 Timer value in EEE function for T_wakeup.
Value from 0~256.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_100_1
Default value is 30 us.
[23:16] R/W 0xa B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tr_100 Timer value in EEE function for T_tx_rate.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_100_1.
Default value is 10 us.
[15:8] R/W 0xf4 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Td_100 Timer value in EEE function for QoS based LPI decision maker T_tx_delay.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_100_2*4.
Default value is 1s.
[7:0] R/W 0xa B_BE_GMAC_EEE_Timer_Tp_100 Timer value in EEE function for TX decision maker T_pause.
Unit: EEE_Timer_Unit_100_1.
Default value is 10 us.
18601894h R_BE_GMAC_EEE_CR4
[31:16] R/W 0x271 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_TX_THR_Giga Set the TX threshold in EEE function. Unit is Byte.
EEE_TX_THR=Round [(T_tx_rate×Rate) / 8 ]. If you set T_tx_rate=10us, Rate=1000Mbps, so set EEE_TX_THR = 0x4E2 (1250bytes).
Default value is 0x271(~half rate).
[15:0] R/W 0x3e B_BE_GMAC_EEE_TX_THR_100 Set the TX threshold in EEE function. Unit is Byte. EEE_TX_THR=Round [(T_tx_rate×Rate) / 8 ]. If you set T_tx_rate=10us, Rate=100Mbps, so set EEE_TX_THR = 0x7D (125bytes).
Default value is 0x3E (~half rate).
18601898h R_BE_GMAC_EEE_LPI_TM0
[31:22] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[21:0] RC 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_RXLPI_Time RX in LPI time. (unit: 1us) (accumulation)Valid as (PORT_MASK[0]==1&&RXTimer_EN==1)
1860189Ch R_BE_GMAC_EEE_LPI_TM1
[31:24] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_eee_stop_txc This bit works in rgmii_1000 and rgmii_100 modes only.
1: gmac halts gmac_rgmii_txc_out when gmac enters lpi.
0: gmac does not halt gmac_rgmii_txc_out when gmac enters lpi.
This bit should always be 1’b0 for RLE0390(原RL6166). Gmac can halt gmac_rgmii_txc_out in the next version.
[22] R/W 1 B_BE_GMAC_eee_trxctl_level The gmac_rgmii_txe and gmac_rgmii_rxdv level when gmac is in lpi.
1: tx_ctl and rx_ctl is high when in lpi.
0: reserved.
[21:0] RC 0 B_BE_GMAC_EEE_TXLPI_Time TX in LPI time. (unit: 1us ) (accumulation)Valid as (PORT_MASK[0]==1&&TXTimer_EN==1)
186018A0h R_BE_GMAC_Dummy_A0
[31:0] RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_A0

[31:0] RW 0xDEADBEEF B_BE_GMAC_Dummy_A4

[31:29] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD

186018D8h R_BE_GMAC_ISR1
[31:29] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[28] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TDU5 Tx Descriptor Ring5 Unavailable:
When set, indicates Tx descriptor Ring5 is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[27] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TDU4 Tx Descriptor Ring4 Unavailable:
When set, indicates Tx descriptor Ring4 is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[26] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TDU3 Tx Descriptor Ring3 Unavailable:
When set, indicates Tx descriptor Ring3 is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[25] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TDU2 Tx Descriptor Ring2 Unavailable:
When set, indicates Tx descriptor Ring2 is unavailable.
Write 1 to clear.
[24] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TDU1 This bit is ISR.tdu
[23:21] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[20] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TOK5 Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last Ring5 descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
[19] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TOK4 Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last Ring4 descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
[18] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TOK3 Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last Ring3 descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
[17] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TOK2 Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last Ring2 descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
[16] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_TOK1 Transmit Interrupt:
Indicates that the DMA of the last Ring1 descriptor of TxIntMitigation number of Tx packet has completed and the last descriptor has been closed.
Write 1 to clear.
[15:6] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
[5] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK6 Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires in Ring6.
Write 1 to clear.
[4] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK5 Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires in Ring5.
Write 1 to clear.
[3] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK4 Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires in Ring4.
Write 1 to clear.
[2] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK3 Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires in Ring3.
Write 1 to clear.
[1] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK2 Receive (Rx) OK:
This interrupt is set either when RxIntMitigation packet is met or RxPktTimer expires in Ring2.
Write 1 to clear.
[0] R/W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_ISR_ROK1 This bit is ISR.rok
186018DCh R_BE_GMAC_INTR
[31:25] RW 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD

186018E0h R_BE_GMAC_HIMR00
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HB1ISR_IND_EN HB1ISR Indicator HIMR (BB1 mailbox indictor)
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HB0ISR_IND_EN HB0ISR Indicator HIMR (BB0 mailbox indictor)
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HCI_AXIDMA_IND_EN HCI AXIDMA HIMR
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HC00ISR_IND_EN HC00ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HC00ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HC00IMR are true, issue INT)
[27] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HD1ISR_IND_EN HD1ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HD1ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HD1IMR are true, issue INT)
[26] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HD0ISR_IND_EN HD0ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HD0ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HD0IMR are true, issue INT)
[25] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HS1ISR_IND_EN HS1ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HS1ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HS1IMR are true, issue INT)
[24] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_HS0ISR_IND_EN HS0ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HS0ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HS0IMR are true, issue INT)
[23:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
186018E4h R_BE_GMAC_HISR00
[31] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HB1ISR_IND HB1ISR Indicator HISR (BB1 mailbox indictor)
[30] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HB0ISR_IND HB0ISR Indicator HISR (BB0 mailbox indictor)
[29] W1C 0 B_BE_GMAC_HCI_AXIDMA_IND HCI AXIDMA HISR
[28] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HC00ISR_IND HC00ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HC00ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HC00IMR are true, issue INT)
[27] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HD1ISR_IND HD1ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HD1ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HD1IMR are true, issue INT)
[26] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HD0ISR_IND HD0ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HD0ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HD0IMR are true, issue INT)
[25] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HS1ISR_IND HS1ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HS1ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HS1IMR are true, issue INT)
[24] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_HS0ISR_IND HS0ISR Indicator HIMR (B_BE_GMAC_HS0ISR and B_BE_GMAC_HS0IMR are true, issue INT)
[23:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GMAC_RSVD RSVD
186018E8h R_BE_GMAC_inTagDropPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inTagDropPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the VLAN-Tag or CPU-Tag will be recorded in this counter.
186018ECh R_BE_GMAC_inRuntDropPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inRuntDropPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Runt packets that are dropped will be recorded in this counter.
186018F0h R_BE_GMAC_inCRCErrPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inCRCErrPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the CRC error will be recorded in this counter.
186018F4h R_BE_GMAC_inIPCSErrPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inIPCSErrPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the IP checksum error will be recorded in this counter.
186018F8h R_BE_GMAC_inUDPCSErrPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inUDPCSErrPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the UDP checksum error will be recorded in this counter.
186018FCh R_BE_GMAC_inTCPCSErrPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inTCPCSErrPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the TCP checksum error will be recorded in this counter.
18601900h R_BE_GMAC_RXErrCNT
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_RXErrCNT 32-bit dot1dTpPortInDiscards RxErr.
18601904h R_BE_GMAC_inETHPDropPkt
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_inETHPDropPkt 32-bit drop counter.
Packets that are dropped due to the unknown ETHP will be recorded in this counter.
18601908h R_BE_GMAC_ifInOctets_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInOctets_H ifInOctets:
32 bits counter. The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters.
Note: count with tag length
1860190Ch R_BE_GMAC_ifInOctets_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInOctets_L ifInOctets:
32 bits counter. The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters.

ifHCInOctets:
64 bits counter. Same as ifInOctets.
Note: count with tag length
18601910h R_BE_GMAC_ifInUCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInUCPkt_H The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were not addressed to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer.
Note: only count valid frame
18601914h R_BE_GMAC_ifInUCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInUCPkt_L The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were not addressed to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer.
Note: only count valid frame
18601918h R_BE_GMAC_ifInMCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInMCPkt_H The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were addressed to a multicast address at this sub-layer (Number of received valid multicast packets).
Note: only count valid frame
Note: Exclude Pause Frame
1860191Ch R_BE_GMAC_ifInMCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInMCPkt_L The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were addressed to a multicast address at this sub-layer (Number of received valid multicast packets).
Note: only count valid frame
Note: Exclude Pause Frame
18601920h R_BE_GMAC_ifInBCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInBCPkt_H The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were addressed to a broadcast address at this sub-layer (Number of received valid broadcast packets).
Note: only count valid frame
18601924h R_BE_GMAC_ifInBCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifInBCPkt_L The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a higher (sub-)layer, which were addressed to a broadcast address at this sub-layer (Number of received valid broadcast packets).
Note: only count valid frame
18601928h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutOctets_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutOctets_H ifOutOctets:
32 bits counter. The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters.

ifHCOutOctets:
64 bits counter. Same as ifOutOctets.
Note: count with tag length
1860192Ch R_BE_GMAC_ifOutOctets_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutOctets_L ifOutOctets:
32 bits counter. The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters.

Note: Byte count of txok pkt.

ifHCOutOctets:
64 bits counter. Same as ifOutOctets.
Note: count with tag length
18601930h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutUCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutUCPkt_H The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were not addressed to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count
18601934h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutUCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutUCPkt_L The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were not addressed to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count
18601938h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutMCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutMCPkt_H The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were addressed to a multicast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count.
Note: Include or Exclude Pause Frame dependent on TX_MULTIPKT_CNT_PAUSE
1860193Ch R_BE_GMAC_ifOutMCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutMCPkt_L The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were addressed to a multicast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count.
Note: Include or Exclude Pause Frame dependent on TX_MULTIPKT_CNT_PAUSE
18601940h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutBCPkt_H
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutBCPkt_H The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were addressed to a broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count.
18601944h R_BE_GMAC_ifOutBCPkt_L
[31:0] R 0 B_BE_GMAC_ifOutBCPkt_L The total number of packets that higher-level protocols requested be transmitted, and which were addressed to a broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those that were discarded or not sent.

Note: discarded or not sent(drain out) packets are count.

[8] R/W 0 B_BE_RSM_L0S_EN enable timer for L0s to recovery

[6] R/W 0 B_BE_DMAC0_EXIT_L1_EN enbable app_req_entr_l1/app_req_exit_l1 from DMAC1 (wd_quota_empty)
[5:4] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_SEL_XFER_PENDING select of app request pending for l1
1: from hardware control
0: from register
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_SEL_REQ_ENTR_L1 select of app request entrying to l1
1: from hardware control
0: from register
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_PCIE_EN_SWENT_L23 Enable SW call EMAC to enter L23
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_SEL_REQ_EXIT_L1 select of app request exiting to l1
1: from hardware control
0: from register

[18:16]: function

18603044h R_BE_PCIE_LAT_CTRL
[31:30] RO 2'b0 RSVD RSVD
[29:24] RW 6'b0 B_BE_ELBI_PHY_REMAP Select EMAC reg mapping to EPHY reg
6’b000000: EMAC 0x3800~0x3BFF equal to EPHY 0x0000~0x03FF
6’b000001: EMAC 0x3800~0x3BFF equal to EPHY 0x0400~0x07FF
6’b000010: EMAC 0x3800~0x3BFF equal to EPHY 0x0800~0x0BFF
…
6’b111111: EMAC 0x3800~0x3BFF equal to EPHY 0x0000~0xFFFF
[23:18] RO 6'b0 RSVD RSVD
[17] RW 1'b1 B_BE_SYS_SUS_L12_EN 1: enable
0: disable
[16] RW 1'b0 B_BE_MDIO_S_EN MDIO serial mode
1: enable
0: disable
serial mode doesn't support byte enable function
[15] RO 1'b0 RSVD RSVD
[14:12] RW 3'b1 B_BE_LDO_T3_LAT the latency for waiting power source is stable
0: 0us (don't use)
1: 5us
2: 10us
3: 15us
4: 20us
5: 25us
6: 30us
7: 35us
[11] RO 1'b0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] RW 3'b1 B_BE_VMAIN_LAT the latency for waiting power source is stable
0: 0us (don't use)
1: 5us
2: 10us
3: 15us
4: 20us
5: 25us
6: 30us
7: 35us
[7:4] RW 4'b1 B_BE_CLK_REQ_LAT Bit[7:4]: EMAC delay setting
0000 : No delay
0001 : 30us
0010 : 50us
0011 : 80us
0100 : 100us
0101 : 120us
0110 : 150us
0111 : 180us
1000 : 200us (hw default value)
1001 : 300us
1010 : 400us
1011 : 500us
1100 : 1ms
1101 : 3ms
1110 : 5ms
1111 : 10ms
[3:2] RO 3'b0 RSVD
[1] RW 1'b0 B_BE_RTK_PM_SEL_OPT the ECO eMAC delay option
0: disable
1: Modified RTK request with additional delay
[0] RW 1'b0 B_BE_CLK_REQ_SEL the clock removel request for PHY is from
0: DWC original signal
1: RTK request with additional delay

[9:8] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:4] R/W 0 B_BE_LBC_TIMER Timer for setting LBC timeout watchdog: 0000: 8 us, 0001: 16 us, 0010: 32 us, 0011: 64 us, 0100: 128 us, 0101: 256 us, 0110: 512 us, 0111: 1 ms, 1000: 2 ms, 1001: 4 ms, 1010~1111: 8 ms
[3:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] W1C 0 B_BE_LBC_FLAG LBC timeout flag. Write “1” to clear.
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_LBC_EN Enable LBC timeout watchdog

186031C4h R_BE_PCIE_DBG_GPO

186031D0h R_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE
[31:11] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_MODE [debug state] state mode
0: LTSSM
1: PM current
2: PM master
3: PM slave
3: PM L1s
[7] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:4] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_GRP select debug group, only 4 units [debug state]
this function is used to setting the maximum continued 3 states want to send a trigger singal
[3] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_MSK [debug state] trigger mask
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_CLR [debug state] clear tirgger
[0] W1S 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_SET [debug state] wirte 1 to begin setting debug state
186031D4h R_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D
[31:23] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[22:16] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D3 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
[15] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[14:8] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D2 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
[7] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D1 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
186031D8h R_BE_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D
[31:22] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[21:16] RO 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D3 [debug state] the state status
[15:14] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[13:8] RO 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D2 [debug state] the state status
[7:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:0] RO 0 B_BE_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D1 [debug state] the state status
18603330h R_BE_PCIE_MIT_TMR
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:4] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_MIT_RX_TMR Timer unit for setting Interrupt Migration RX timer.
00: 1 ms, 01: 2 ms, 10: 4 ms, 11: 8 ms
Combine with0x3334[15:8] to obtain time value of RX timer.
ex : this reg. = 0, 0x3334[15:8] = 3, RX timer = 1msx3 = 6 ms
Note: Timeout variation is +- 1 timer unit
[3:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_MIT_TX_TMR Timer unit for setting Interrupt Migration TX timer.
00: 1 ms, 01: 2 ms, 10: 4 ms, 11: 8 ms
Combine with0x3334[7:0] to obtain time value of TX timer.
ex : this reg. = 0, 0x3334[7:0] = 3, TX timer = 1msx3 = 6 ms
Note: Timeout variation is +- 1 timer unit
18603334h R_BE_PCIE_MIT_CNT
[31:24] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_RX_MIT_CNT When the buffered RX packet number is matched, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable RX counter
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_TX_MIT_CNT When the buffered TX packet number is matched, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable TX counter
[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_RX_MIT_TMR_CNT Multiple value of RX timer unit.
Combine with 0x3330[5:4] to obtain time value of RX timer.
When the counting time matches or exceeds this setting, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable RX timer
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PCIE_TX_MIT_TMR_CNT Multiple value of TX timer unit.
Combine with 0x3330[1:0] to obtain time value of TX timer.
When the counting time matches or exceeds this setting, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable TX timer

18603C00h R_BE_HCI_LA_FLOW_CONTROL
[31:1] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_GO 0: disable HCI_LA record, 1: start to wait trigger/ record
18603C04h R_BE_HCI_LA_RECORD_SET
[31:8] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:6] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_REC_MODE set record mode:
00: record ends when triggerred
00: record half before/ after triggerred
00: record starts when triggerred
[5:4] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_DEC_MODE set ANC_clk division rate
[3] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_ADP_MODE 1: enable data capture adaptive mode
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_PAT_MODE_1 0: direct trigger mode 1: pattern trigger mode
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_PAT_MODE_0 0: input dbg data normal, 1: input dbg data inverse
18603C08h R_BE_HCI_LA_MEM_SET
[31:8] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_HCI_LA_IND_MEM_OFFSET Indirect memory read offset
18603C10h R_BE_HCI_LA_TRIG_SET

1: enable trigger condition. 0: disable trigger condition

18606000h R_BE_RXAGG_0_V1
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_EN Enable RX Aggregation function
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_DMA_STORE Enable dma agg
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_SW_EN enable SW control RXAGG stop
[28] W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_SW_TRIG SW write 1 to break RXAGG
[27:24] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_PKTNUM_TH RXDMA aggregation packet number threshold
[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_TIMEOUT_TH RXDMA aggregation timeout threshold : unit : 32us(usb), 1us(sdio)
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_LEN_TH When RXDMA_AGG_EN is set, then RXDMA will inform host to receive RX packets, when packet page number in RXFF exceeds the threshold set in RXDMA_AGG_PG_TH register.
Unit : 4k for USB aggregation mode 1k for Rx DMA aggregation mode
18606004h R_BE_RXAGG_1_V1
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_TIMEOUT_UNIT 0:32us ,1:1us
[30:18] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[17:16] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_LEN_TH_HIGH_V1 When r_rxagg_en is set, then RXDMA will inform host to receive RX packets, when packet page number in RXFF exceeds the threshold set in r_rxagg_len_th register. Unit : 1k
[15:8] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:5] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_SML_PKT_SIZE_V1 The parameter of target pkt length during rx agg process, where target pkt length = 64x2^(B_BE_PKTLEN_PARA-1) bytes.
[4:0] R/W 0 B_BE_RXAGG_SML_PKTNUM_TH RXDMA breaks rx agg when the amount of aggregated pkts of length smaller than target pkt length exceeds this threshold.

[6] W 0 B_BE_RX_LEN_RCLR RX len fifo read clear
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_LEN_WCLR RX len fifo write clear
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_DATA_FIFO_RST RX data fifo pulse reset
