18607000h R_BE_GT0_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
18607004h R_BE_GT0_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607008h R_BE_GT1_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT1_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT1_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT1_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT1_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT1_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
1860700Ch R_BE_GT1_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT1_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607010h R_BE_GT2_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT2_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT2_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT2_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT2_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT2_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
18607014h R_BE_GT2_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT2_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607018h R_BE_GT3_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT3_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT3_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT3_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT3_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT3_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
1860701Ch R_BE_GT3_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT3_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607020h R_BE_TOPOFF_DBG_SEL
[31:4] R/W 0 rsvd
[3:0] R/W 0 B_BE_TOPOFF_DBG_SEL 4'b0: dbg0_reg
others: RSVD
18607024h R_BE_SORT_CTRL
[31:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC1_SORT_EN CMAC1 sorting enable
When B_BE_CMAC1_SORT_EN=1, HW will sort CMAC1 bcn early timers and ps timers.
If CMAC1 is inactive, B_BE_CMAC1_SORT_EN must be set to 0.
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC0_SORT_EN CMAC0 sorting enable
When B_BE_CMAC0_SORT_EN=1, HW will sort CMAC0 bcn early timers and ps timers.
18607040h R_BE_GT4_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT4_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT4_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT4_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT4_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT4_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
18607044h R_BE_GT4_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT4_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607048h R_BE_GT5_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT5_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT5_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT5_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT5_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT5_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
1860704Ch R_BE_GT5_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT5_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607050h R_BE_GT6_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT6_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT6_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT6_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT6_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT6_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
18607054h R_BE_GT6_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT6_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607058h R_BE_GT7_CTRL
[31] W1T 0 B_BE_GT7_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_GT7_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT7_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_BE_GT7_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_BE_GT7_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
1860705Ch R_BE_GT7_CNT
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_BE_GT7_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_BE_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_BE_GT_DATA=1, B_BE_GT_CNT will be count down from B_BE_GT_DATA. When B_BE_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.

18607160h R_BE_H2CREG_CTRL
[31:1] R/W 0 rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_H2CREG_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger H2C REG interrupt to FW.
(After receiving H2C, FW must write 0 to B_BE_H2C_REG_TRIGGER to let the host know the data has received)
18607164h R_BE_C2HREG_CTRL
[31:1] R/W 0 rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_C2HREG_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger C2H REG interrupt to the host.
(After receiving C2H, the host must write 0 to B_BE_C2H_REG_TRIGGER to let FW know the data has received)
18607170h R_BE_CPWM
[31:16] R/W 0 rsvd
[15] R/W 0 B_BE_CPWM_TOGGLE If FW toggle this bit, CPWM HISR will be issued
[14:0] R/W 0 B_BE_CPWM_VAL CPWM value
18607180h R_BE_ANACLK_CAL_CTRL
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_ANACLK_CAL_EN 1: polling CK Calibration enable and indicate busy when calibration is on going
0: calibration is done
[30:26] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[25:24] R/W 0 B_BE_ANACLK_SEL 00: caliabration of 12M for 16 cycles
01: calibration of 32K for 8 cycles
10, 11: rsvd
[23:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_ANACLK_CAL_RPT the calibration value of ANA clock where the calibration clock is MAC clock.
18607190h R_BE_OTP_PARA
[31:6] R/W 0 RSVD
[5:4] R/W 1 B_BE_OTP_PWE_H_PARA OTP PWE high level parameter.
0 : 10us~11us / 1 : 12us~13us / 2 : 14us~15us / 3 : 15us~16us
[3:2] R/W 0 RSVD
[1:0] R/W 1 B_BE_OTP_PWE_L_PARA OTP PWE low level parameter.
0 : 1us~2us / 1 : 2us~3us / 2 : 3us~4us / 3 : 5us~6us

[21] R/W 0 RSVD
[20] R/W 0 RSVD
[19] R/W 0 RSVD
[18] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_CMAC1_WTM_TO_INT_EN Enable the interrupt source that wakeup due to CMAC1 wake timer.
[17] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_CMAC0_WTM_TO_INT_EN Enable the interrupt source that wakeup due to CMAC0 wake timer.
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_DMAC_WTM_TO_INT_EN Enable the interrupt source that wakeup due to DMAC wake timer.

[1] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_BT_Mailbox_INT_EN Enable Mailbox INT (BT Mailbox to WIFI)
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_FS_RPWM_INT_EN Enable RPWM interrupt source (wakeup from HWLPS)
18607804h R_BE_FWD1ISR
[31] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[30] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[29] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[28] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[27] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[26] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[25] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[24] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[23] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[22] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[21] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[20] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[19] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[18] R/W1C 0 B_BE_FS_CMAC1_WTM_TO_INT When CMAC1_WTM_CNT >= (CMAC1_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME), HW issue this interrupt.
[17] R/W1C 0 B_BE_FS_CMAC0_WTM_TO_INT When CMAC0_WTM_CNT >= (CMAC0_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME), HW issue this interrupt.
[16] R/W1C 0 B_BE_FS_DMAC_WTM_TO_INT When DMAC_WTM_CNT >= (DMAC_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME), HW issue this interrupt.

[14] R/W1C 0 RSVD

[5] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[4] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[3] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[2] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[1] R/W1C 0 B_BE_FS_BT_Mailbox_INT The interrupt is issued while BT trigger Mailbox (BT Mailbox to WIFI)
[0] R/W1C 0 B_BE_FS_RPWM_INT The interrupt is issued while RPWM toggle bit changed. (wakeup from HWLPS)
18607808h R_BE_FSMIMR
[31] R/W 0 RSVD
[30] R/W 0 RSVD
[29] R/W 0 RSVD
[28] R/W 0 RSVD
[27] R/W 0 RSVD
[26] R/W 0 RSVD
[25] R/W 0 RSVD
[24] R/W 0 RSVD
[23] R/W 0 RSVD
[22] R/W 0 RSVD
[21] R/W 0 RSVD
[20] R/W 0 RSVD
[19] R/W 0 RSVD
[18] R/W 0 RSVD
[17] R/W 0 RSVD
[16] R/W 0 RSVD
[15] R/W 0 RSVD
[14] R/W 0 RSVD
[13] R/W 0 RSVD
[12] R/W 0 RSVD
[11] R/W 0 RSVD
[10] R/W 0 RSVD
[9] R/W 0 RSVD
[8] R/W 0 RSVD
[7] R/W 0 RSVD
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx period end event. No wake WCPU.
0: Disable Rx period end event. No wake WCPU.
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_RX_BCN_TO_CNT_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx bcn timeout counter event wake WCPU
0: Disable Rx bcn timeout counter event wake WCPU
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_RX_MATCH_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx match event wake WCPU
0: Disable Rx match event wake WCPU
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_HIOE_ERR_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable HIOE error event wake WCPU
0: Disable HIOE error event wake WCPU
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_OTHERS_WAKE_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable others wake event wake WCPU
0: Disable others wake event wake WCPU
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_FSM_TIMER_TO_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable other timer timeout event wake WCPU
0: Disable other timer timeout event wake WCPU
[0] R/W 0 rsvd
1860780Ch R_BE_FSMISR
[31] RO 0 RSVD
[30] RO 0 RSVD
[29] RO 0 RSVD
[28] RO 0 RSVD
[27] RO 0 RSVD
[26] RO 0 RSVD
[25] RO 0 RSVD
[24] RO 0 RSVD
[23] RO 0 RSVD
[22] RO 0 RSVD
[21] RO 0 RSVD
[20] RO 0 RSVD
[19] RO 0 RSVD
[18] RO 0 RSVD
[17] RO 0 RSVD
[16] RO 0 RSVD
[15] RO 0 RSVD
[14] RO 0 RSVD
[13] RO 0 RSVD
[12] RO 0 RSVD
[11] RO 0 RSVD
[10] RO 0 RSVD
[9] RO 0 RSVD
[8] RO 0 RSVD
[7] RO 0 RSVD
[6] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_RP_END_INT & FS_RP_END_INT_EN).
When this event happened and IMR = 1, FSM load the register of R_BE_HIOE_STR_ADDR & R_BE_HIOE_END_ADDR to HIOE STR/END address, then load the register of R_BE_BKP_HIOE_CTRL to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL.
[5] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_RX_BCN_TO_CNT_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_RXBCN_TO_CNT_INT. & FS_RXBCN_TO_CNT_INT._EN)
When BCN_TO_CNT > BCN_TO_CNT_THD, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[4] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_RX_MATCH_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from
( (FS_WWLAN_INT & FS_WWLAN_INT_EN) | (FS_BCN_HIT_INT & FS_BCN_HIT_INT_EN) )
When Rx packet match wakeup pattern or Rx Bcn hit (IE_HIT/BCAID_HIT/UNIAID_HIT), issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[3] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_HIOE_ERR_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_HIOE_ERR_INT & FS_HIOE_ERR_INT_EN).
When HIOE timeout or format error, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[2] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_OTHERS_WAKE_EVENT_ISR When others wake source triggered and imr is enabled, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
Others wake event include
- All (FWISRs & FWIMRs) in AON domain
- - (FWD1ISR[15:0] & FWD1IMR[15:0]) in PON domain
[1] RO 0 B_BE_FSM_TIMER_TO_EVENT_ISR When others timer timeout triggered and corresponding imr =1, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU.
Timers include
- GTimer
- PSTimer
[0] RO 0 rsvd
18607810h R_BE_TM_BKP_RES_CTRL
[31:28] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC1_SORT_ROLE After CMAC1 sorting done, HW updated this field.
CMAC1_SORT_EN[3]:
0: BCN early
1: PS timer
CMAC1_SORT_EN[2:0]
0: port 0 bcn early
1: port 1 bcn early
…
4: port 4 bcn early
OR
0: PS timer 0
1: PS timer 1
…
6: PS timer 6
[27:24] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC0_SORT_ROLE After CMAC0 sorting done, HW updated this field.
CMAC0_SORT_EN[3]:
0: BCN early
1: PS timer
CMAC0_SORT_EN[2:0]
0: port 0 bcn early
1: port 1 bcn early
…
4: port 4 bcn early
OR
0: PS timer 0
1: PS timer 1
…
6: PS timer 6
[23:16] RO 0 B_BE_SORTING_ROLE After sorting done, HW updated this field.
SORTING_ROLE[7]:
0: SORTING_ROLE is invalid.
1: SORTING_ROLE is valid.

SORTING_ROLE[5:4]:
2b’00: DMAC
2b’01: CMAC0
2b’10: CMAC1

SORTING_ROLE[3:0]:
DMAC:C
0: GTimer 0
1: GTimer 1
…
7: GTimer 7

CMAC 0/1:
[3]:
0: BCN early
1: PS timer
[2:0]
0: port 0 bcn early
1: port 1 bcn early
…
4: port 4 bcn early
OR
0: PS timer 0
1: PS timer 1
…
6: PS timer 6
[15:13] R/W 0 RSVD Reserved.
[12] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC1_SCH_RES_EN CMAC1 scheduler-related restore enable. Set by FW.
FW set this bit to 1 before starting to restore CMAC1 scheduler-related registers.
FW set this bit to 0 after restored CMAC1 scheduler-related registers.
[11] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC1_RES_EN CMAC1 restore enable. Set by FW.
FW set this bit to 1 before starting to restore CMAC1 unscheduled-related registers.
FW set this bit to 0 after restored CMAC1 unscheduled-related registers.
[10] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC0_SCH_RES_EN CMAC0 scheduler-related restore enable. Set by FW.
FW set this bit to 1 before starting to restore CMAC0 scheduler-related registers.
FW set this bit to 0 after restored CMAC0 scheduler-related registers.
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC0_RES_EN CMAC0 restore enable. Set by FW.
FW set this bit to 1 before starting to restore CMAC0 unscheduled-related registers.
FW set this bit to 0 after restored CMAC0 unscheduled-related registers.
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_DMAC_RES_EN DMAC restore enable. Set by FW.
FW set this bit to 1 before starting to restore DMAC timers and registers.
FW set this bit to 0 after restored DMAC timers and registers.
[7] RO 0 B_BE_TM_WAKE_IND LPS Wake Timer wake indication
1: Wake by LPS Wake Timer
0: Not wake by LPS Wake Timer
[6] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC1_BKP_EN_STS CMAC1 backup enable status. Set by HW.
HW set this bit to 1 when TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER = 1.
HW set this bit to 0 when
1. FW clear CMAC1_RES_EN or
2. pre-check fail.
[5] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC0_BKP_EN_STS CMAC0 backup enable status. Set by HW.
HW set this bit to 1 when TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER = 1.
HW set this bit to 0 when
1. FW clear CMAC0_RES_EN or
2. pre-check fail.
[4] RO 0 B_BE_DMAC_BKP_EN_STS DMAC backup enable status.
Set by HW.
HW set this bit to 1 when TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER = 1.
HW set this bit to 0 when
1. FW clear DMAC_RES_EN or
2. pre-check fail.
[3] RW 0 B_BE_PRE_CHK_DONE LPS Wake Timer Pre-check Done.
Set by HW. HW set this bit to 1 when timer sorting and pre check flow done.
Cleared by FW. FW set this bit to 0 before set TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER to 1.
[2] RW 0 B_BE_PRE_CHK_VALID LPS Wake Timer Pre-check valid bit.
If PRE_CHK_DONE = 1 and PRE_CHK_VALID = 1, pre-check success.
If PRE_CHK_DONE = 1 and PRE_CHK_VALID = 0, pre-check fail.
[1] R/W 0 RSVD Reserved.
[0] RW 0 B_BE_TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER Timer backup and sorting trigger bit.
Set by FW. FW set this bit to 1 to trigger HW to do timer sorting and pre check flow.
Cleared by HW. HW set this bit to 0 when timer sorting and pre check flow done.
18607814h R_BE_PRE_CHK_CTRL
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_PRE_CHK_THD LPS Wake Timer Pre-check threshold
Sort the minimum value of off domain timer. If the value >= PRE_CHK_THD, LPS_PRE_CHK_VALID will be set to 1. Else, LPS_PRE_CHK_VALID reset to 0
Unit: 1us
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PRE_WAKE_TIME LPS Wake Timer Pre-wakeup time.
When (TM_BKP_EN =1) & (PRE_CHK_VALID =1), LPS_WTM_CNT start counting. TIMERS_TO_EVENT will be issued when
LPS_WTM_CNT >= (LPS_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME)
Unit: 1us
18607818h R_BE_DMAC_WTM_SC
[31:0] RW 0 B_BE_DMAC_WTM_SC DMAC wake timer sorting value.
HW trigger wake event when DMAC_WTM_CNT >= (DMAC_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME).
1860781Ch R_BE_DMAC_WTM_CNT
[31:0] RO 0 B_BE_DMAC_WTM_CNT DMAC wake timer counter.
Unit: 1 us.
When DMAC_BKP_EN_STS = 1, this counter is counting.
When DMAC_BKP_EN_STS = 0, this counter will stop counting and reset to 0.
18607820h R_BE_TSF_32K_SEL
[31:18] R/W 0 rsvd
[17] RO 0 B_BE_TSF_CLK_STABLE Indicate that TSF 32k/dmac_clk is stable
1’b1= TSF is stable in 32K,
1’b0 = TFS is stable in dmac_clk
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_CKSL_WLTSF TSF clock select:
1’b1 = TSF is 32K,
1’b0 = TSF is dmac_clk.
[15:9] R/W 0 rsvd
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_32K_SRC_SEL 32k clock source select:
1’b1 = 32.768K,
1’b0 = 32K.
[7:0] R/W 0xA0 B_BE_US_TIME_VALUE cycle counts of dmac_clk for 1us.
18607824h R_BE_MAC_PWR_STS
[31:2] R/W 0 RSVD Reserved.
[1] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC1_PWR_STS CMAC1 power status.
1: CMAC1 is power on. SYM_WLCMAC1_PC_EN = 1.
0: 0: CMAC1 is power off. SYM_WLCMAC1_PC_EN = 0.
[0] RO 0 B_BE_CMAC0_PWR_STS CMAC0 power status.
1: CMAC0 is power on. SYM_WLCMAC0_PC_EN = 1.
0: CMAC0 is power off. SYM_WLCMAC0_PC_EN = 0.
18607828h R_BE_CMAC0_WTM_SC
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC0_WTM_SC CMAC 0 wake timer sorting value.
HW trigger wake event when CMAC0_WTM_CNT >= (CMAC0_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME).
1860782Ch R_BE_CMAC0_WTM_CNT
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC0_WTM_CNT CMAC 0 wake timer counter.
Unit: 1 us.
When CMAC0_BKP_EN_STS = 1, this counter is counting.
When CMAC0_BKP_EN_STS = 0, this counter will stop counting and reset to 0.
18607830h R_BE_CMAC1_WTM_SC
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC1_WTM_SC CMAC 1 wake timer sorting value.
HW trigger wake event when CMAC1_WTM_CNT >= (CMAC1_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME).
18607834h R_BE_CMAC1_WTM_CNT
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_CMAC1_WTM_CNT CMAC 1 wake timer counter.
Unit: 1 us.
When CMAC1_BKP_EN_STS = 1, this counter is counting.
When CMAC1_BKP_EN_STS = 0, this counter will stop counting and reset to 0.
18607838h R_BE_MIN_SORT_VAL
[31:0] RO 0 B_BE_MIN_SORT_VAL Minimum sorting value for all (include DMAC/CMAC0/CMAC1).
18607840h R_BE_HIOE_END_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_HIOE_END_ADDR FSM load this register to B_PL_INST_END_ADDR in below case:
LPS resume & (B_BE_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
(B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
18607844h R_BE_HIOE_STR_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_HIOE_STR_ADDR FSM load this register to B_PL_INST_STR_ADDR in below case:
- LPS resume & (B_BE_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
- (B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
18607848h R_BE_BKP_HIOE_CTRL
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_BKP_HIOE_CTRL FSM load this register to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL in below case:
(B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_BE_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
[15:0] R/W 0 rsvd
1860784Ch R_BE_RES_HIOE_CTRL
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_RES_HIOE_CTRL FSM load this register to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL in below case:
LPS resume & (B_BE_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_BE_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
[15:0] R/W 0 rsvd

1860840Ch R_BE_DMAC_P0_RSVD_00C
[31:0] RO 16'hdeadbeef B_BE_DMAC_P0_RSVD_00C
18608410h R_BE_LTR_CTRL_0
[31:19] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[18] $RW 1'b0 B_BE_LTR_REQ_FW Write 1 to request update LTR FW index
[17:16] RW 2'b00 B_BE_LTR_IDX_FW LTR FW index value
[15:11] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] RW 3'b101 B_BE_LTR_IDLE_TIMER_IDX LTR IDLE timer index. If TX DMA is idle and RX DMA is idle and RX packet buffer is empty, a timer gets started. After time set by the index expired, LTR idle message will be sent.
0: 100T; 1: 1000T; 2: 10000T; 3: 20000T; 4: 40000T; 5: 80000T; 6: 160000T; 7: 320000T. Unit: DMAC clock cycle.
[7:2] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] RW 1'b1 B_BE_LTR_WD_NOEMP_CHK 1: Enable WD non-empty check condition for LTR idle -> active.
[0] RW 1'b0 B_BE_LTR_HW_EN 1: Enable hardware LTR state machine. If you want to disable HW LTR, use 0x1600[2] to disable HW LTR decision.
18608414h R_BE_LTR_CTRL_1
[31:28] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[27:16] RW 12'h028 B_BE_LTR_RX1_TH RX threshold for CMAC 1. Unit: page. Send LTR active msg if CMAC 1 RX used page is over this threshold.
[15:12] RW 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:0] RW 12'h028 B_BE_LTR_RX0_TH RX threshold for CMAC 0. Unit: page. Send LTR active msg if CMAC 0 RX used page is over this threshold.
18608418h R_BE_LTR_IDLE_LATENCY
[31:2] RW 30'b0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] RW 2'b11 B_BE_LTR_IDLE_IDX LTR idle idx for HW LTR.
1860841Ch R_BE_LTR_ACTIVE_LATENCY
[31:2] RW 30'b0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] RW 2'b0 B_BE_LRT_ACT_IDX LTR act idx for HW LTR.
18608420h R_BE_DMAC_TABLE_CTRL
[31] RW 0 B_BE_HWAMSDU_PADDING_MODE 0: The last AMSDU doesn’t add padding for 4 bytes alignment.(default follow spec)
1: The last AMSDU adds padding for 4 bytes alignment.
[30:27] RW 0 RSVD
[26:16] RW 11'h100 B_BE_MACID_MPDU_PROCESSOR_OFFSET DMAC_TABLE mpdu processor Base address, unit : 8byte
[15:13] RW 0 RSVD
[12] RW 0 B_BE_DMAC_ADDR_MODE 0: Virtual address mode
1: Normal (direct) address mode
[11] RW 0 B_BE_DMAC_CTRL_INFO_SER_IO
[10:0] RW 0 B_BE_DMAC_CTRL_INFO_OFFSET DMAC_TABLE Ctrl Info Base address, unit : 8byte

18608440h R_BE_LTR_DECISION_CTRL
[31:17] RO 15'b0 RSVD RSVD
[16] RO 1'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_DRV_VLD 1: indicate lat_ltr_idx_drv is valid
[15:14] RO 2'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_DRV current latched ltr_idx_drv setting
[13] RO 1'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_FW_VLD 1: indicate lat_ltr_idx_fw is valid
[12:11] RO 2'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_FW current latched ltr_idx_fw setting
[10] RO 1'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_HW_VLD 1: indicate lat_ltr_idx_hw is valid
[9:8] RO 2'b0 B_BE_LAT_LTR_IDX_HW current latched ltr_idx_hw setting
[7] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_LTR_REQ_DRV write 1 to request driver ltr
[6:5] R/W 2'b0 B_BE_LTR_IDX_DRV set latency idx by driver
[4:2] R/W 3'b0 B_BE_LTR_EN_PORT [4] : enable driver port
[3] : enable fw port
[2] : enable hw port
[1:0] R/W 2'b11 B_BE_LTR_SPACE_IDX LTR space index. It sets the minimal space between two LTR messages. Recommend set the minimal space to 512us per PCIE Spec. Other value is only for debug purpose.
0: 10 us; 1: 128 us; 2: 256 us; 3: 512us.
18608444h R_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX0
[31:0] R/W 32'h880b880b B_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX0 LTR latency value for idx 0. Shall be the smallest value in idx 0~3.
18608448h R_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX1
[31:0] R/W 32'b0 B_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX1 LTR latency value for idx 1. Shall set to larger value than idx 0.
1860844Ch R_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX2
[31:0] R/W 32'b0 B_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX2 LTR latency value for idx 2. Shall set to larger value than idx 1.
18608450h R_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX3
[31:0] R/W 32'h0 B_BE_LTR_LATENCY_IDX3 LTR latency value for idx 3. Shall be the largest value in idx 0~3.
18608454h R_BE_CURRENT_LTR_LATENCY
[31:0] RO 32'b0 B_BE_CUR_LTR_LATENCY Current LTR latency value.

[4] RW 1'b0 B_BE_FS_WWLAN_INT_EN Enable HW detect WWLAN event interrupt source
[3] RW 1'b0 B_BE_FS_PLD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q1_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue1 of PLD_ENG.

[0] RW 1'b0 B_BE_FS_WD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q0_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue0 of WD_ENG.

[10] W1C 1'd0 B_BE_PLE_BURST_NUM_ERR
[9] W1C 1'd0 B_BE_PLE_NULL_PKT_ERR
[8] W1C 1'd0 B_BE_PLE_FLOW_CTRL_ERR 代表曾經發生過"CMAC要求dispatcher上去host抓PLD，但是因為DMAC quota暫時用完，而沒辦法抓PLD下來"的狀況發生過。這在PLD on host mode其實很常發生，代表的意義就是如果TX有出現ZLD，那可以調大DMAC PLD quota，可能可以改善TX ZLD的現象
[7] W1C 1'd0 B_BE_HDR_DMA_TIMEOUT_ERR
[6] W1C 1'd0 B_BE_HDR_RX_TIMEOUT_ERR

If this bit is set to 1, then NULL page can't be written, but can be read. If this bit is set to 0, then NULL page can be written and read

[2] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_FRZTMR_MODE datchn FSM freeze timer mode selction
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_QUEMGN_FRZTMR_MODE quemgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_BUFMGN_FRZTMR_MODE bufmgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
18608C38h R_BE_WDE_ERR_IMR
[31:30] R/W 0x3 DUMMY DUMMY
[29] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn page index cam receoves CAM entry request, but packet ID is existed in CAM
[28] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn d_addr is larger than max page offset supported by HW
[27] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_RRDY_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn unsupporting read wait state detects d_rvalid=1 & d_rready=0 during read transfer
[26] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for datchn FSM is frozen over 32us
[25] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_NULLPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for accessing NULL page
[24] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_DATCHN_ARBT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for d_req is deasserted before transfer is completed
[23] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_QUEMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quemgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[22] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_NXTPKTLL_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for nxtpktllt access address is error
[21] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_PREPKTLLT_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for prepktllt access address is error
[20] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_ENQ_PKTCNT_NVAL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is negative value during dequeue operation
[19] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_ENQ_PKTCNT_OVRF_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is overflow during enqueue operation
[18] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_QUE_SRCQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_srcprtid, q_srcqueid or q_macid
[17] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_QUE_DSTQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_dstprtid, q_dstqueid or q_macid
[16] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_QUE_CMDTYPE_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_cmdtype
[15] R/W 1 DUMMY DUMMY
[14] R/W 1 DUMMY DUMMY
[13] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFMGN_MRG_SZLMT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects sum of page number of merge buffer strpktid and endpktid is over HW limiation
[12] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFMGN_MRG_QTAID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects quota ID of merge buffer strpktid and endpktid is not equal
[11] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFMGN_MRG_ENDPKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects merge buffer endpktid is invalid in pktinfo table
[10] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_ERR_BUFMGN_MRG_STRPKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects merge buffer strpktid is invalid in pktinfo table
[9] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[8] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_GETNPG_PGOFST_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for next page offset is 0 during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[7] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_GETNPG_STRPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for start page indx is NULL during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[6] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFREQ_SRCHTAILPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for searching tail page index to NULL during buffer request
[5] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFRTN_SIZE_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for return buffer size > current used page number
[4] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFRTN_INVLD_PKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for return buffer packet ID is invalid in pktinfo table
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFREQ_UNAVAL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for request buffer size > available page number
[2] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFREQ_SIZELMT_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is over HW limitation
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFREQ_SIZE0_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is 0
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_WDE_BUFREQ_QTAID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quota ID of buffer request is error

18608D10h R_BE_WDE_DBG_FUN_INTF_CTL
[31] $W1S 0 B_BE_WDE_DFI_ACTIVE Write 1 to enable debug function interface, and the HW clear it as action is completed.
This bit is 1 to indicate action is not completed, and B_BE_WDE_DFI_DATA is invalid.
[30:20] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[19:16] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_DFI_TRGSEL Debug function selection.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_DFI_ADDR Debug function input index. E.g. page index, packet ID… etc.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
18608D14h R_BE_WDE_DBG_FUN_INTF_DATA
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_DFI_DATA Debug function output data.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.

18608D80h R_BE_WDE_Q_STATUS_CFG
[31:4] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[3] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[2:0] R/W 0 B_BE_WDE_Q_STATUS_SEL Determine B_BE_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL will show empty status of which MACID
0: MACID31~0 queue empty
1: MACID63~32 queue empty
2: MACID95~64 queue empty
3:MACID127~96 queue empty
else: RSVD
18608D84h R_BE_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL
[31:0] RO 0xFFFFFFFF B_BE_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL Indicate the queue of MACID is empty.
1: empty
0: non empty

18609018h R_BE_PLE_DATCHN_CTL
[31:9] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[8] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_NULLPG_WRPORT NULL page write protection.
If this bit is set to 1, then NULL page can't be written, but can be read. If this bit is set to 0, then NULL page can be written and read
[7:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
1860901Ch R_BE_PLE_FUNC_RSTCTL
[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy

Write 1 to generate 1T sync reset pulse to reset datchn of DLE port interface 07

Write 1 to generate 1T sync reset pulse to reset datchn of DLE port interface 06

[0] $W1S 0 B_BE_PLE_GEN_PIF00 DLE port interface 00 function reset.

18609020h R_BE_PLE_DBGERR_LOCKEN
[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF07 DLE port interface 07 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 07, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF06 DLE port interface 06 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 06, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF05 DLE port interface 05 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 05, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF04 DLE port interface 04 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 04, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF03 DLE port interface 03 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 03, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF02 DLE port interface 02 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 02, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF01 DLE port interface 01 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 01, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF00 DLE port interface 00 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 00, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn

[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy

[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
1860902Ch R_BE_PLE_WORD02C
[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
18609030h R_BE_PLE_ERRFLAG_MSG
[31:0] RO 0 B_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_MSG Error flag message
18609034h R_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_CFG_NUM1
[31] W1C 0 B_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_VLD Indicate B_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX and B_BE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX are valid
[30:28] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[27:24] R 0 B_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX Bit index for first error flag
[23:21] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[20:16] R 0 B_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX Master index for first error flag. See sheet "1st_errlfag_idx" for detail
[15:3] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_FRZTMR_MODE datchn FSM freeze timer mode selction
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_QUEMGN_FRZTMR_MODE quemgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_FRZTMR_MODE bufmgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
18609038h R_BE_PLE_ERR_IMR
[31:30] R/W 0x3 DUMMY Dummy
[29] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn page index cam receoves CAM entry request, but packet ID is existed in CAM
[28] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn d_addr is larger than max page offset supported by HW
[27] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_RRDY_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn unsupporting read wait state detects d_rvalid=1 & d_rready=0 during read transfer
[26] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for datchn FSM is frozen over 32us
[25] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_NULLPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for accessing NULL page
[24] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_ARBT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for d_req is deasserted before transfer is completed
[23] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_QUEMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quemgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[22] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_NXTPKTLL_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for nxtpktllt access address is error
[21] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_PREPKTLLT_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for prepktllt access address is error
[20] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_ENQ_PKTCNT_NVAL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is negative value during dequeue operation
[19] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_ENQ_PKTCNT_OVRF_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is overflow during enqueue operation
[18] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_QUE_SRCQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_srcprtid, q_srcqueid or q_macid
[17] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_QUE_DSTQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_dstprtid, q_dstqueid or q_macid
[16] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_QUE_CMDTYPE_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_cmdtype
[15] R/W 1 DUMMY DUMMY
[14] R/W 1 DUMMY DUMMY
[13] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_MRG_SZLMT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects sum of page number of merge buffer strpktid and endpktid is over HW limiation
[12] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_MRG_QTAID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects quota ID of merge buffer strpktid and endpktid is not equal
[11] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_MRG_ENDPKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects merge buffer endpktid is invalid in pktinfo table
[10] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_MRG_STRPKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects merge buffer strpktid is invalid in pktinfo table
[9] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[8] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_GETNPG_PGOFST_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for next page offset is 0 during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[7] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_GETNPG_STRPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for start page indx is NULL during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[6] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFREQ_SRCHTAILPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for searching tail page index to NULL during buffer request
[5] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFRTN_SIZE_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for return buffer size > current used page number
[4] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFRTN_INVLD_PKTID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for return buffer packet ID is invalid in pktinfo table
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFREQ_UNAVAL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for request buffer size > available page number
[2] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFREQ_SIZELMT_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is over HW limitation
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFREQ_SIZE0_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is 0
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_PLE_BUFREQ_QTAID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quota ID of buffer request is error
1860903Ch R_BE_PLE_ERR_FLAG_ISR
[31] W1C 0 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_EVT7_ERR Reserved
[30] W1C 0 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_EVT6_ERR Reserved
[29] W1C 0 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR Error flag status bit for B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR_INT_EN
[28] W1C 0 B_BE_PLE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR Error flag status bit forB_BE_PLE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR_INT_EN

18609110h R_BE_PLE_DBG_FUN_INTF_CTL
[31] $W1S 0 B_BE_PLE_DFI_ACTIVE Write 1 to enable debug function interface, and the HW clear it as action is completed.
This bit is 1 to indicate action is not completed, and B_BE_PLE_DFI_DATA is invalid.
[30:20] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[19:16] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_DFI_TRGSEL Debug function selection.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_DFI_ADDR Debug function input index. E.g. page index, packet ID… etc.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
18609114h R_BE_PLE_DBG_FUN_INTF_DATA
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_PLE_DFI_DATA Debug function output data.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.

18609404h R_BE_WDRLS_WORD004
[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
18609408h R_BE_WDRLS_CFG
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_WDRLS_DIS_AGAC Disable auto clock gating control for functional gating-clock gck_func. If this bit is set to 1, then functional gating clock gck_func is always enabled.
[30:16] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[15:8] R/W 0x40 B_BE_RLSRPT_BUFREQ_TO PLE_DLE buffer request wait time limit, unit in 32us. If buffer request waiting time is large then this threshold, then HW will assert error interrupt to 0x1860_8504[5]. If this value is set to 0, then wait timer is disabled.
[7:6] R/W 0 B_BE_RLSRPT_BUFREQ_TO_SEL PLE DLE buffer request wait timer unit selection
2'h0: 32us, 2'h1: 256us, 2'h2~2'h3: 1024us
[5:2] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[1:0] R/W 0 B_BE_WDRLS_MODE Report configuration mode to control report type classification
0: PCIE release report + WLAN CPU release report
1: USB/SDIO release report + WLAN CPU release report
2: Only WLAN CPU release report
3: RSVD
1860940Ch R_BE_WDRLS_WORD00C
[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy

[9] R/W 0x1 B_BE_WDRLS_RPT0_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for rptgen0 FSM is frozen over 32us

[7:6] R/W 0x3 DUMMY Dummy
[5] R/W 0x1 B_BE_WDRLS_PLEBREQ_PKTID_ISNULL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for PLE_DLE reply NULL packet ID for buffer request

[3] R/W 0x1 DUMMY Dummy

18609440h R_BE_RLSRPT0_CFG0
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_RLSRPT0_FWRLS FW release field of Rx descriptor (offset 0 [58]) for report type 0
[30:24] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[23:16] R/W 0x3 B_BE_RLSRPT0_FWD_TRGT Forward target field of Rx decriptor (offset 0 [55:48]) for type 0.
[1:0]: Forward method to Host CPU0
[3:2]: Forward method to Host CPU1
[5:4]: Forward method to Data CPU
[7:6]: Forward method to WLAN CPU
Forward method:
2'h0: Not need to forward
2'h1: Partial
2'h2: RSVD
2'h3: Full
[15:11] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[10:8] R/W 0 B_BE_RLSRPT0_PID PLE_DLE port ID for report type 0

18609444h R_BE_RLSRPT0_CFG1
[31:28] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[27:24] R/W 0xF B_BE_RLSRPT0_FLTR_MAP Report generation control for each WDE_DLE release queue.
If bit is set to 1 for report generation, else bypass report generation
[0]: WDE_DLE release queue - TXOK
[1]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for retry limit
[2]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for lifetime timeout
[3]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for MACID drop
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_RLSRPT0_TO Report life time, unit in 32us. If this value is 0, then lifetime function is disabled.
[15:8] R/W 0 DUMMY DUMMY
[7:0] R/W 0x1 B_BE_RLSRPT0_AGGNUM Maximum aggregation packet number for report type 0.

18609620h R_BE_CH_INFO_CFG0
[31:30] R/W 0 RSVD dummy
[29:24] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_QID Destination queue ID for report
[23] R/W 0 RSVD dummy
[22:20] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_PRTID The ch info destination queue ID
port id 0 & queue id 2: host
port id 0 & queue id 0xB: wlcpu
[19:18] R/W 1 B_BE_CH_INFO_REQ_TIMSET Clock cycle number for bb_ch_info_req, unit in DMAC clock cycle
2'h0: 1 T
2'h1: 2 T
2’h2: 3 T
[17:16] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_RPT_SZ The channel info MAC segment size.
(including RX descriptor)
0: 128 bytes
1: 256 bytes
2: 512 bytes
3: 1024 bytes
[15:12] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_INTVL Shortest interval between consequent channel info. request, unit in 1us.
4'h0: disable
4'h1: 1 us
4’h2: 2 us
4’h3: 3 us
4’h4: 4 us
4'h5: 5 us
4'h6: 6 us
4'h7: 7 us
4'h8: 8 us
4'h9: 9 us
4'ha: 10 us
4'hb: 11 us
4'hc: 12 us
4'hd: 13 us
4'he: 14 us
4'hf: 15 us
[11:10] R/W 1 B_BE_CH_INFO_DATA_WTLMT Wait time limit selection for bb channel info. data, dma_rdrdy
2'h0: disable, 2'h1: 1us, 2'h2: 2us, 2'h3: 3us

P.S. Due to wait timer resolution is 1us, timer count to timeouot maybe delay 1us. For exmaple, the value is set to 2'h1 for 1us wait timeout, but timeout may be delay to 2us, actually
[9:8] R/W 1 B_BE_CH_INFO_RPT_WTLMT Wait time limit selection for bb channel info report ready, bb_ch_info_on
2'h0: disable, 2'h1: 2us, 2'h2: 4us, 2'h3: 8us

P.S. Due to wait timer resolution is 1us, timer count to timeouot maybe delay 1us. For exmaple, the value is set to 2'h1 for 2us wait timeout, but timeout may be delay to 3us, actually
[7] R/W 0 RSVD
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_CMAC_SEL CMAC channel info. interface selection
1'b0: CMAC0, 1'b1: CMAC1
[5] RO 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_STOP Indicate the channel info module is in the stop state
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_STOP_REQ Write 1 to stop channel info module
[3] RO 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_ON Indicate DMAC is in the get-ch-info progress. Show bb_ch_info_en status
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_IFFO_BYP_ENDWTON2L FSM return to IDLE state whether need wait low active of bb_ch_info_on
0: diable, 1: bypass
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_BYP_STRWTON2H FSM request report whether need wait high active of bb_ch_info_on
0: check, 1: bypass
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_CH_INFO_EN Enable ch info function.

2'h1: 64 bytes

(MUST set before disable DFS report)

18609660h R_BE_LA_CFG
[31] R/W 0 DUMMY dummy
[30:24] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_TRIG_TIME_VAL Delay time for dump finish after la_stop assertion
6'h0: No delay, 6'h1: 1 TU, 6'h2: 2TU, …
where TU is defined by B_BE_LA_TRIG_TU_SEL
[23:20] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_TRIG_TU_SEL Time unit selection for B_BE_LA_TRIG_TIME_VAL,
TU (time unit) = 2^ B_BE_LA_TRIG_TU_SEL
4'h0: 1us (2^0), 4'h1: 2us (2^1), …, 4'hf: 32768us (2^15)
[19:16] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_BUF_SEL LA ring buffer size selection
4’h0: 64KB
4’h1: 128KB
4’h2: 192KB
4’h3: 256KB
4’h4: 320KB
Misc: RSVD
[15] R/W 0 DUMMY dummy
[14:8] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_BUF_BNDY Start boundary of PKTBUFFER for LA ring buffer, unit in 8Kbytes
[7:6] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_TO_VAL Timeout value for 1st la_stop wait timer
2'h0: 1s, 2'h1: 2s, 2'h2: 4s, 2'h3: 8s
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_TO_EN Set 1 to enable wait timer for 1st la_stop
1: enable, 0: disable
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_RESTART_EN Set 1 to allow la_trig to re-strart LA data dump
1: enable, 0: disable
Note: this function is disabled as B_BE_LA_TO_EN=1
[3] $W1S 0 B_BE_LA_TRIG_START Set 1 to trigger LA data dump start
1: Wait to dump start, 0: Start dump
[2:1] R/W 0 DUMMY dummy
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_LA_FEN LA function enable
0: disable, 1: enable
18609664h R_BE_LA_STATUS
[31:20] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[19:17] RO 0 B_BE_LA_SW_FSMST Current status
3'h0: LA idle
3'h1: LA start
3'h2: LA finish stop
3'h3: LA finish timeout
3'h4: LA re-start
[16] RO 0 B_BE_LA_BUF_RNDUP Indicate B_BE_LA_BUF_WPTR has round up
1: round up, 0: No round up
[15:0] RO 0 B_BE_LA_BUF_WPTR LA ring buffer write pointer. LA data dump finish address = (B_BE_LA_BUF_WPTR -1)

18609A00h R_BE_PKTIN_SETTING
[31:5] RO 0 RSVD Reseved
[4:2] R/W 0x1 B_BE_AMSDU_MAX_FS_NUM The max searching number of AMSDU forward search:
0x0: 0
0x1: 5
0x2: 15
0x3: 25
0x4: 35
0x5: 45
[1] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_PKTIN_CLK_GATING_DIS Control PKTIN module clk gating disable
0:clok gating
1:disable clock gating
18609A04h R_BE_HWAMSDU_CTRL
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD Reseved
[5:3] R/W 0x3 B_BE_MAX_AMSDU_NUM Break condition of max HW AMSDU number
0x0 : 1 msdu
0x1 : 2 msdus
0x2 : 3 msdus
0x3 : 4 msdus
0x4 : 5 msdus
0x5 : 6 msdus
0x6 : 7 msdus
0x7 : 8 msdus
[2] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SINGLE_AMSDU When BIT_SINGLE_AMSDU = 1 and AMSDU field =1 of tx descriptor, the packets will use AMSDU format at packet and more packets. When BIT_SINGLE_AMSDU = 0, hardware will update WD.HWAMSDU to 0, and not generate Single AMSDU packet.
0: Disable
1: Enable
[1] R/W 0x0 B_BE_AMSDU_FS_ENABLE 0: disable AMSDU forward search
1: ebable AMSDU forward search
[0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_HWAMSDU_EN 0: Disable HWAMSDU function
1: Enable HWAMSDU function
18609A08h R_BE_HWAMSDU_STATUS
[31] R/W1C 0x0 B_BE_AMSDU_PKT_SIZE_ERR packet size of AMSDU packet > amsdu_pkt_size_th
[30] R/W1C 0x0 B_BE_AMSDU_EN_ERR Assert when r__hwamsdu_en is disable and WD.AMSDU is enabled in packet (WD Body)
[29] R/W1C 0x0 B_BE_AMSDU_ADDR_INFO_ERR Per pkt last addr info (MSDU_LS , LS)  !=  (1,0)
[28] R/W1C 0x0 B_BE_AMSDU_RD_SEL_Q_ERR
[27:0] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
18609A14h R_BE_TXPKTIN_CTRL
[31:26] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[25] W1C 0 B_BE_C1P4_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC1 Port 4 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC1 Port 4 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[24] W1C 0 B_BE_C1P3_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC1 Port 3 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC1 Port 3 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[23] W1C 0 B_BE_C1P2_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC1 Port 2 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC1 Port 2 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[22] W1C 0 B_BE_C1P1_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC1 Port 1 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC1 Port 1 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[21] W1C 0 B_BE_C1P0_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC1 Port 0 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC1 Port 0 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[20] W1C 0 B_BE_C0P4_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC0 Port 4 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC0 Port 4 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[19] W1C 0 B_BE_C0P3_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC0 Port 3 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC0 Port 3 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[18] W1C 0 B_BE_C0P2_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC0 Port 2 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC0 Port 2 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[17] W1C 0 B_BE_C0P1_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC0 Port 1 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC0 Port 1 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[16] W1C 0 B_BE_C0P0_TXPKTIN_STS CMAC0 Port 0 TxPktIn interrupt.
When driver sends a CMAC0 Port 0 packet into PKTIN, HW issue this interrupt. Clear by FW.
[15] RW 0 B_BE_TXPKTIN_CTRL_EN 1: Enable TXPKTIN generate interrupt
When host sends a packet into PKTIN, PKTIN module check if WDinfo’s QSEL & MULTIPORT_ID match the B_AX_C(m)P(n)_TXPKTIN_En, PKTIN issues interrupt to inform MCU.
0: Disable TXPKTIN generate interrupt
[14:10] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_C1P4_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC1 Port 4 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_C1P3_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC1 Port 3 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_C1P2_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC1 Port 2 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_C1P1_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC1 Port 1 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_C1P0_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC1 Port 0 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_C0P4_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC0 Port 4 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_C0P3_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC0 Port 3 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_C0P2_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC0 Port 2 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_C0P1_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC0 Port 1 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_C0P0_TXPKTIN_EN Mask of CMAC0 Port 0 TxPktIn interrupt. Set by FW:
1: Enable
0: Disable

18609B00h R_BE_HDR_SHCUT_SETTING
[31:5] RSVD RSVD RSVD RSVD
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_ADDR_MLD_TO_LIK 1: emable HW security header 0: disable HW security header
enable header convertor to translat MLO address to link address
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_TX_HW_SEC_HDR_EN 1: emable HW security header 0: disable HW security header
enable header convertor to insert security header if securitry type == CCMP\GCMP\TKIP
[2] R/W 1 B_BE_TX_MAC_MPDU_PROC_EN 1: enable TX MAC header convert
0: disable TX MAC header convert
[1] R/W 1 B_BE_TX_HW_ACK_POLICY_EN 1: enable HW update ACK policy
0: disable HW update ACK policy
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_TX_HW_SEQ_EN 1: enable HW update sequence number
0: disable HW update sequence number

[31:16] RO 0x0 B_BE_TX_PTKOUT_CNT Count value of packet forwarding from MPDU TX Processor
[15:0] RO 0x0 B_BE_TX_PKTIN_CNT Count value of packet forwarding to MPDU TX Processor
18609BF0h R_BE_MPDU_TX_ERR_ISR
[31:10] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[9] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_KSRCH_ERR
[8] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_NW_TYPE_ERR
[7] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_LLC_PRE_ERR
[6] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_ETH_TYPE_ERR
[5] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR MPDU processor detect a tx packet in 802.3
format with a header length which is less than 14 bytes
[4] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_OFFSET_ERR Offset value for Security Engine is underflow (negative)
[3] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_ERR Get a packet with zero length mpdu (MPDU_desc.MPDU-SIZE == 0)
in TX descriptor
[2] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_ERR In USB/SDIO mode get next packet ID from TX descriptor is zero
[1] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_ERR Get null packet ID from MPDU TX Queue
[0] W1C 0 B_BE_TX_TIMEOUT_ERR mpdu_tx state machine does not return IDLE and time out
18609BF4h R_BE_MPDU_TX_ERR_IMR
[31:10] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[9] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_KSRCH_ERR_EN tx key search fail
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_NW_TYPE_ERR_EN network type is no link
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_LLC_PRE_ERR_EN llc[23:0] != 24’h03AAAA
[6] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_ETH_TYPE_ERR_EN when packet is eth2, type field is less than 1536; when packet is 802.3, length field is larger than 1536
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR_INT_EN B_BE_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[4] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_OFFSET_ERR_INT_EN B_BE_TX_OFFSET_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_INT_EN B_BE_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_INT_EN B_BE_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_INT_EN B_BE_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_TIMEOUT_ERR_EN B_BE_TX_TIMEOUT_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
18609BF8h R_BE_MPDU_TX_DBG_V1
[31:9] RSVD RSVD RSVD RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_BE_MPDU_TX_DBGEN_V1 Enable debug MPDU TX Processor port output
[7:2] RSVD RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_MPDU_TX_CKEN_DIS disable mpdu tx processor clock gating
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_MPDU_TX_DLAST_V1 Enable MPDU TX processor to toggle d_last else output low
18609C00h R_BE_MPDU_PROC
[31:30] RO 0 RSVD
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_PORT_SEL 0: To dispatcher and follow RXD.FWD
1: WLCPU/DataCPU CPU_IO option
[28:27] R/W 0 B_BE_WPKT_WLANCPU_QSEL Queue info (WLCPU.RX_Channel or CPU_IO.channel)
[26:25] R/W 0 B_BE_WPKT_DATACPU_QSEL Queue info (DataCPU.RX_Channel or CPU_IO.channel)
[24] R/W 0 B_BE_WPKT_FW_RLS For WLCPU and DataCPU path used
0: Release by dispatcher
1: Release by WLCPU/DataCPU, Dispatcher module decide full packet in CPU.Rx channel or partial packet in CPU.Rx channel and full packet in CPU_IO style.
[23:16] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_RPKT Report packet type(RXD.FWD != 0) forwarding destination in bypass mode when B_BE_RXFWD_EN = 0
[15:8] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_WPKT wifi packet forwarding destination in bypass mode when B_BE_RXFWD_EN = 0
[7:6] RO 0 RSVD
[5:4] R/W 0 B_BE_RXFWD_PRIO 2'b00: host0 first
2'b01: host1 first
2'b10: WLMCU first
2'b11 DMCU first
[3] R/W 1 B_BE_RXFWD_EN 0: Bypass mode
1: Enable rx forwarding function
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_DROP_NONDMA_PPDU if macinfo[31] == 0, drop ppdu status report
[1] RO 0 RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_APPEND_FCS 0: remove fcs from packet
18609C04h R_BE_FWD_AF0
[31:24] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_DELTS DELTS packet filled RxD.Target
RxD.Target
Bit[1:0]: Forwarding to HOSTCPU 0
Bit[3:2]: Forwarding to HOSTCPU 1
Bit[5:4]: Forwarding to DataCPU
Bit[7:6]: Forwarding to WLCPU
[23:16] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_ADDTS_RES ADDTS_RES packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
[15:8] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_ADDTS_REQ ADDTS_REQ packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
[7:0] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_CSA Channel switch announcement packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
18609C08h R_BE_FWD_AF1
[31:24] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_NCW Notify channel width packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

[7:0] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_ADDBA_REQ ADDBA_REQ packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

[31:24] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_HT_CBFM HT compressed beamforming packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
[23:16] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_CSI CSI packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
[15:8] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_OP_MODE Operating mode notification packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
[7:0] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_GID_MGNT GroupID packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

[31:24] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_CRC32 CRC32 error packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

[31:24] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_CTRL3 ACTN_CTRL3 packet filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

18609C18h R_BE_FWD_ACTN1
[31:24] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_ACTN1 Setting action_1 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.
[23:16] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_CAT1 Setting category_1 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.
[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_ACTN0 Setting action_0 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_CAT0 Setting category_0 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.
18609C1Ch R_BE_FWD_ACTN2
[31:24] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_ACTN3 Setting action_3 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.

[15:8] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_ACTN2 Setting action_2 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.
[7:0] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_ACTN_CAT2 Setting category_2 field of MGN frame, let Rx forwarding circuit parsing.

[31:24] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_TF3 Trigger frame type = 3 filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.

18609C30h R_BE_HW_PPDU_STATUS
[31:26] R/W 12 B_BE_FWD_RPKTTYPE RxD.packet type setting rule.
[25:23] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_PPDU_PRTID Port ID setting, it satisfies by B_BE_FWD_RPKTTYPE condition.
[22] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_PPDU_FW_RLS The PPDU path
0: Report release by HW(Use P_AXIDMA channel)
1: Report release by FW((partial packet in P_AXIDMA channel, full packet in CPUIO))
[21:16] R/W 0 B_BE_FWD_PPDU_QUEID Queue ID setting, it satisfies by B_BE_FWD_RPKTTYPE condition.
[15:8] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_OTHER_RPKT RXD.Target setting, it not satisfies by
B_BE_FWD_RPKTTYPE, PPDU_STATUS and Wifi packets
[7:0] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_PPDU_STAT The packet type is PPDU or B_BE_FWD_RPKTTYPE setting filled RxD.Target, the rule is the same with B_BE_FWD_DELTS register.
18609C34h R_BE_FWD_IE
[31:16] RO 0 RSVD
[15:8] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_IE_HIT Determine the target when BCN IE hit
BIT[0], 0: Don't check, 1: To HOST0
BIT[1], 0: Don't check, 1: To HOST1
BIT[2], 0: Don't check, 1: To DataCPU
BIT[3], 0: Don't check, 1: To WLCPU
[7:0] R/W 3 B_BE_FWD_IE_NO_HIT Determine the target when BCN IE does not hit
BIT[0], 0: Don't check, 1: To HOST0
BIT[1], 0: Don't check, 1: To HOST1
BIT[2], 0: Don't check, 1: To DataCPU
BIT[3], 0: Don't check, 1: To WLCPU

[31] R/W 0 B_BE_EN_CUT_AMSDU Enable AMSDU_CUT
[30] R/W 0 B_BE_CUT_AMSDU_CHKLEN_EN HW check MSDU length enable
[29] R/W 0 B_BE_CA_CHK_ADDRCAM_EN Cut A-MSDU when rx packet's addrcam hit = 1
[28:25] RO 0 RSVD
[24] R/W 1 B_BE_MPDU_CUT_CTRL_EN
[23:16] R/W 0x14 B_BE_CUT_AMSDU_CHKLEN_L_TH HW check cut MSDU length range. (Low threshold)
[15:0] R/W 0x1500 B_BE_CUT_AMSDU_CHKLEN_H_TH HW check cut MSDU length range. (High threshold)
18609C98h R_BE_CUT_AMSDU_CTRL_2
[31:20] RO 0 B_BE_MSDU_DROP_SN sequence number of the AMSDU which trigger R_AX_REG_ERROR_FLAG
[19] W1C 0 B_BE_MSDU_DROP_SN_CLR Write 1 to clear B_AX_MSDU_DROP_SN
[18:17] R/W 0 B_BE_EXTRA_SHIFT leave extra space from previous msdu packet to make payload starting in 4/8 bytes alignment
0: no shift
1: 4 bytes aligned
2: 8 bytes aligned
3: reserve
[16:1] RO 0 RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_REPLACE_RXD_DA
18609C9Ch R_BE_CUT_AMSDU_ERROR_FLAG
[31:3] RO 0 RSVD
[2] W1C 0 B_BE_AMSDU_2_LEN
[1] W1C 0 B_BE_AMSDU_2_SMALL
[0] W1C 0 B_BE_AMSDU_2_LARGE
18609CA0h R_BE_RX_NAT2P5_CTRL0
[31:27] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_LEN_3 Comparing length of NAT2.5 comparation group #3
[26:17] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_OFFSET_3 Comparing offset of NAT2.5 comparation group #3
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_VALID_3 Enable NAT2.5 comparation group #3
[15:11] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_LEN_2 Comparing length of NAT2.5 comparation group #2
[10:1] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_OFFSET_2 Comparing offset of NAT2.5 comparation group #2
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_VALID_2 Enable NAT2.5 comparation group #2
18609CA4h R_BE_RX_NAT2P5_CTRL1
[31:27] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_LEN_1 Comparing length of NAT2.5 comparation group #1
[26:17] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_OFFSET_1 Comparing offset of NAT2.5 comparation group #1
[16] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_VALID_1 Enable NAT2.5 comparation group #1
[15:11] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_LEN_0 Comparing length of NAT2.5 comparation group #0
[10:1] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_OFFSET_0 Comparing offset of NAT2.5 comparation group #0
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_NAT2P5_RX_CP_VALID_0 Enable NAT2.5 comparation group #0
18609CA8h R_BE_RX_CTRL
[31:7] RO 0 RSVD
[6:1] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_IP_OFFSET
[0] R/W 1 B_BE_RX_CTRL_EN
18609CB8h R_BE_WOW_CTRL
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[5] R/W 0 B_BE_WOW_HCI This bit is set to allow wake host as wake on
wlan event occurring
[4:3] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_WOW_DROP This bit is set to drop packet, when not match
wake up pattern/magic packet
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_WOW_WOWEN WoW function on or off
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_WOW_FORCE_WAKEUP Let Wakeup PIN be controllable by FW/Driver.
18609CC0h R_BE_HDR_CNV_CTRL
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:4] R/W 0 B_BE_HDR_INFO 0: Disable add driver info
1: Add BSSID in driver info
2: Add 802.11 header in driver info
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_HC_ADDR_HIT_EN valid when B_BE_RX_HDR_CNV_EN = 1
1: only convert header when rxd.addr_cam_vld = 1
[2] RO 0 B_BE_RX_ADDR_LINK_TO_MLO 0: disable RX MLO address translation
1: enable RX MLO address translation
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_HDR_CNV valid when B_BE_RX_HDR_CNV_EN = 1
0: convert to 802.3
1: convert to ether2
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_HDR_CNV_EN Enable RX header conversion(802.11 -> 802.3/ether2)
18609CC4h R_BE_HDR_CNV_VLAN_0_1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_1 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 1
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_0 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 0
18609CC8h R_BE_HDR_CNV_VLAN_2_3
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_3 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 3
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_2 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 2
18609CCCh R_BE_HDR_CNV_VLAN_4_5
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_5 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 5
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_4 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 4
18609CD0h R_BE_HDR_CNV_VLAN_6_7
[31:16] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_7 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 7
[15:0] R/W 0 B_BE_VLAN_TAG_6 Valid when B_BE_VLAN_TAG_EN & DCTRL.VLAN_SEL = 6
18609CD4h R_BE_RX_CLSSIFY_MASK_0
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_CLASSIFY_MASK_L
18609CD8h R_BE_RX_CLSSIFY_MASK_1
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD
[5:0] R/W 0 B_BE_RX_CLASSIFY_MASK_H
18609CE0h R_BE_MGCPKT
[31:8] RO 0 RSVD
[7] R/W 0 B_BE_MGCPKT_IOT_EN
[6:5] RO 0 RSVD
[4:0] R/W 0x16 B_BE_MGCPKT_RPET_NBR
18609CE4h R_BE_DESIGN_VERSION
[31:0] RO 0x20210901 B_BE_MPDU_DESIGN_VERSION
18609CE8h R_BE_STATISTIC0
[31:16] RO 0 B_BE_RX_PKTOUT_CNT Count value for packet forward from mpdu rx processor
[15:0] RO 0 B_BE_RX_PKTIN_CNT Count value for packet forward to mpdu rx processor
18609CECh R_BE_STATISTIC1
[31:16] RO 0 RSVD
[15:0] RO 0 B_BE_RX_PKTDROP_CNT Count value for drop by mpdu rx processor
18609CF0h R_BE_MPDU_RX_ERR_ISR
[31:3] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[2] W1C 0 B_BE_FWDRSVD_ERR_ISR RXD.IDC[2] = 1 which indicted packet would forward to Data CPU
[1] W1C 0 B_BE_TIMEOUT_ERR_ISR
[0] W1C 0 B_BE_GETPKTID_ERR_ISR Get a null buffer ID during the buffer operations
18609CF4h R_BE_MPDU_RX_ERR_IMR
[31:4] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[3] R/W 0 B_BE_RPT_ERR_INT_EN Receive Report Packet error event interrupt enable
[2] R/W 0 B_BE_FWDRSVD_ERR_INT_EN RXD.IDC[2] = 1 which indicted packet would forward to Data CPU error event interrupt enable
[1] R/W 0 B_BE_TIMEOUT_ERR_INT_EN
[0] R/W 0 B_BE_GETPKTID_ERR_INT_EN Get a null buffer ID during the buffer operations error event interrupt enable
18609D00h R_BE_SEC_ENG_CTRL
[31] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_SEC_ENG_EN Enable MAC security engine. 1: Enable, 0: Disable.
[30] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CCMP_SPP_MIC 1: use QC[7] to compute CCMP's MIC, 0: not use QC[7] to compute CCMP's MIC.
[29] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CCMP_SPP_CTR 1: use QC[7] to compute CCMP's CTR, 0: not use QC[7] to compute CCMP's CTR.
[28] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_ACC Select access method of security CAM.
0: Direct access, 1: Indirect access.
[27:26] R/W 2'b0 B_BE_WMAC_SEC_PN_SEL GCM SMS4 use
[25] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_WMAC_SEC_MASKIV GCM SMS4 use
[24] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_WAPI_SPEC 1: new wapi spec, 0: old wapi spec
[23] R/W 4'b0 B_BE_REVERT_TA_RA_MLD_EN revert TA RA In MLD 1: Enable, 0: Disable.
[22:20] R/W 4'b0 RSVD
[19:16] R/W 4'b0 B_BE_SEC_DBG_SEL
[15] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CAM_FORCE_CLK stop keycam dynamic clock
[14] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_SEC_FORCE_CLK stop sec dynamic clock
[13] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_SEC_RX_SHORT_ADD_ICVERR
[12] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_sram_io_prot
[11] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_SEC_PRE_ENQUE_TX Tx partial mode enable
[10] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CLK_EN_CGCMP Enable CCMP and GCMP engine clock. 1: Enable, 0: Disable.
Note: CCMP128, CCMP256, GCMP128, GCMP256 and BIP need to enable it.
[9] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CLK_EN_WAPI Enable WAPI engine clock. 1: Enable, 0: Disable.
Note: WAPI need to enable it.
[8] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_CLK_EN_WEP_TKIP Enable WEP and TKIP engine clock. 1: Enable, 0: Disable.
Note: WEP and TKIP need to enable it.
[7:6] R/W 2'b0 RSVD RSVD
[5] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_BMC_MGNT_DEC Determine whethere management packet with broadcast or multicast
will be decrypted or not.
1: Decrypt, 0: Not decrypt.
[4] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_UC_MGNT_DEC Determine whethere management packet with unicast
will be decrypted or not.
1: Decrypt, 0: Not decrypt.
[3] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_MC_DEC Determine whethere multicast will be decrypted or not.
1: Decrypt, 0: Not decrypt.
[2] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_BC_DEC Determine whethere broadcast will be decrypted or not.
1: Decrypt, 0: Not decrypt.
[1] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_SEC_RX_DEC Enable RX decryption function. 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[0] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_SEC_TX_ENC Enable TX encryption function. 1: Enable, 0: Disable
18609D04h R_BE_SEC_MPDU_PROC
[31:9] R/W 30'b0 RSVD RSVD
[8] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_dbg_engine_sel dbg port choose sec0 or sec1
[7] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_stop_rx_pkt_handle
[6] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_stop_tx_pkt_handle
[5] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_queue_foward_sel
[4] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_resp1_protect
[3] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_resp0_protect
[2] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_tx_active_protect
[1] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_APPEND_ICV Append ICV. 1: Append, 0: Not append.
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_APPEND_MIC Append MIC. 1: Append, 0: Not append.
18609D10h R_BE_SEC_CAM_ACCESS
[31:16] R/W 16'b0 B_BE_SEC_TIME_OUT timeout 1us threshold
[15] $W1C 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_POLL Write '1' to trigger indirect access and read '0' to check access done.
Note: Only valid when B_AX_SEC_CAM_ACCESS = 1.
[14] R/W 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_RW Indirect access security CAM. 1: Write, 0: Read.
[13] $W1C 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_ACC_FAIL Cannot access security CAM.
[12:11] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:0] R/W 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_OFFSET Security CAM offset for indirect access. Unit: 4bytes.
18609D14h R_BE_SEC_CAM_RDATA
[31:0] RO 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_RDATA Security CAM read content
18609D18h R_BE_SEC_CAM_WDATA
[31:0] R/W 0 B_BE_SEC_CAM_WDATA Security CAM write content

[31:30] R/W 0 B_BE_TX_TIMEOUT_SEL TX time out select

[15:4] RO 12'hfff B_BE_RX_LAST_PKTID Rx last pkt id
[3:1] RO 0 RSVD
[0] W1C 0 B_BE_SEC_ERROR_BE_BYPASS bypass current parser operation

18609F00h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_COM_CFG
[31:24] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD000_BYTE3 Dummy
[23:18] R/W 6'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD000_BYTE2 Dummy
[17:16] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_WDRLS_REASON4_DSTSEL WD releason reason4 (No report) destination port/queue ID selection
2'h0~1: wdrls queue4, 2'h2: cpuio queue0, 2'h3: cpuio queue1
[15:14] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_WDRLS_REASON3_DSTSEL WD releason reason3 (MACID drop) destination port/queue ID selection
2'h0~1: wdrls queue3, 2'h2: cpuio queue0, 2'h3: cpuio queue1
[13:12] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_WDRLS_REASON2_DSTSEL WD releason reason2 (Drop for lifetime timeout) destination port/queue ID selection
2'h0~1: wdrls queue2, 2'h2: cpuio queue0, 2'h3: cpuio queue1
[11:10] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_WDRLS_REASON1_DSTSEL WD releason reason1 (Drop for retry limit) destination port/queue ID selection
2'h0~1: wdrls queue1, 2'h2: cpuio queue0, 2'h3: cpuio queue1
[9:8] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_WDRLS_REASON0_DSTSEL WD releason reason0 (TXOK) destination port/queue ID selection
2'h0~1: wdrls queue0, 2'h2: cpuio queue0, 2'h3: cpuio queue1
[7:4] R/W 2'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD000_BYTE0 Dummy
[3:2] R/W 2'h3 B_BE_COM_FSM_FRZTMR_TUSEL Time unit selection for cmdpsr FSM frozen timer
2'h0: 32us, 2'h1: 128us, 2'h2: 512us, 2'h3: 1024us
[1:0] R/W 2'h2 B_BE_COM_CLKFREQ_SEL Clock frequency selection
2'h0: 40Mhz, 2'h1: 80Mhz, 2'h2~2'h3: 160Mhz

[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_COM_EM_1STCMD_IS_END Error mask for first command is END command. If this bit is cleared and first command from cmac_ptcl is END command, then HW set error flag ISR r_Bx_ISR_1STCMD_IS_ERR. If this bit is set, then HW bypass this check.

18609F30h R_BE_DBG_FUN_INTF_CTL
[31] $'W1S 1'b0 B_BE_DFI_ACTIVE Write 1 to enable debug function interface, and then HW clear it as action is completed. This bit is 1 to indicate action is not completed, and r_DFI_DATA is invalid.
[30:24] R/W 7'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD030_BYTE3 Dummy
[23:20] R/W 4'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD030_BYTE2 Dummy
[19:16] R/W 4'h0 B_BE_DFI_TRGSEL Debug function selection.
[15:0] R/W 16'h0 B_BE_DFI_ADDR Debug function input index.
18609F34h R_BE_DBG_FUN_INTF_DATA
[31:0] R/W 32'h0 B_BE_DFI_DATA Debug function output data.
18609F38h R_BE_DBG_CTL_TXPKT
[31:16] R/W 16'h0 B_BE_TPC_DBG1_SEL Debug port 1 selection
[15:0] R/W 16'h0 B_BE_TPC_DBG0_SEL Debug port 0 selection
18609F3Ch R_BE_TPC_DBG_OUT
[31:16] $RO 16'h0 B_BE_TPC_DBG1_OUT Debug port 1 output
[15:0] $RO 16'h0 B_BE_TPC_DBG0_OUT Debug port 0 output
18609F40h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_B0_CFG
[31:25] R/W 7'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD040_BYTE3 Dummy
[24] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_B0_PATC_OPT_BRKOFF Option to break off packet airtime countdown as there is no pending packet in PLE_DLE for cmac_dma
[23:16] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD040_BYTE2 Dummy
[15:8] R/W 8'h37 B_BE_B0_ATCPAR_REFTU_VAL Reference time unit value for HW to transform one-byte-transfer-time to parameter of packet airtime counter, unit in ns. E.g. it is set to 55ns, then HW calculates N bytes transfered per 55ns as the parameter of packet airtime counter. Packet airtime counter still decreases N byte per 50ns, thus airtime counter timeout value is approximate to 50/55 * oringal packet tx time
*Note this value is range from min='d50 to max='d100
[7:1] R/W 7'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD040_BYTE0 Dummy
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B0_DIS_ACGC Disable auto clock gating control for clock of txpktctl_b0
18609F44h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_B0_CTL
[31:24] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD044_BYTE3 Dummy
[23:16] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD044_BYTE2 Dummy
[15:8] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD044_BYTE1 Dummy
[7:2] R/W 6'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD044_BYTE0 Dummy
[1] RO 1'b1 B_BE_B0_CTLST_IDLE Indicate txpktctl FSM stays in IDLE state
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B0_STOP_REQ Stop function enable. As this bit is set, HW will stop any handshake with CMAC, and start to do flush flow. After flush flow is completed, HW will be into IDLE state until stop function disable.
18609F48h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_B0_PRELD_CFG0
[31] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B0_PRELD_FEN Band0 preload function enable
[30:26] R/W 5'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD048_BYTE3 Dummy
[25:16] R/W 10'h0 B_BE_B0_PRELD_USEMAXSZ Maximum used buffer size of total preload packet's payload, unit in 64bytes. This is soft constraints for total used buffer size. If total used buffer size is larger then this threshold, then preload buffer is deemed full and don't allow adding new packet to preload cam even if cam is not full
[15:13] R/W 3'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD048_BYTE1 Dummy

'h0: No limit, 'h1: 1us, 'h2: 2us, …, 'hf:15us
[7:0] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_B0_PRELD_NXT_RSVMINSZ Minimux reserved buffer size to allow next tx preload, unit in 64 bytes. This is soft constraints for available tx buffer page number. If available tx buffer page number is small than this threshold, then stop to fethch next tx packet's payload

[2] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD074_BIT02 Dummy
[1] $RO 1'b0 B_BE_B0_DBGERR_LOCKSTS As debug error mode is enabled, it indicate debug error lock status
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B0_DBGERR_LOCKEN Debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is band0 error flag, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of txpktctl_cmdpsr

18609F80h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_B1_CFG
[31:25] R/W 7'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD080_BYTE3 Dummy
[24] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_B1_PATC_OPT_BRKOFF Option to break off packet airtime countdown as there is no pending packet in PLE_DLE for cmac_dma
[23:16] R/W 8'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD080_BYTE2 Dummy
[15:8] R/W 8'h37 B_BE_B1_ATCPAR_REFTU_VAL Reference time unit value for HW to transform one-byte-transfer-time to parameter of packet airtime counter, unit in ns. E.g. it is set to 55ns, then HW calculates N bytes transfered per 55ns as the parameter of packet airtime counter. Packet airtime counter still decreases N byte per 50ns, thus airtime counter timeout value is approximate to 50/55 * oringal packet tx time
*Note this value is range from min='d50 to max='d100
[7:1] R/W 7'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD080_BYTE0 Dummy
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B1_DIS_ACGC Disable auto clock gating control for clock of txpktctl_b0
18609F84h R_BE_TXPKTCTL_B1_CTL

[7:2] R/W 6'h0 B_BE_DUMMY_WD084_BYTE0 Dummy
[1] RO 1'b1 B_BE_B1_CTLST_IDLE Indicate txpktctl FSM stays in IDLE state
[0] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_B1_STOP_REQ Stop function enable. As this bit is set, HW will stop any handshake with CMAC, and start to do flush flow. After flush flow is completed, HW will be into IDLE state until stop function disable.

[25:16] R/W 10'h0 B_BE_B1_PRELD_USEMAXSZ Maximum used buffer size of total preload packet's payload, unit in 64bytes. This is soft constraints for total used buffer size. 1860A330h R_BE_TXL_SRAM_DATA
[31:0] RW 0 B_BE_SRAM_DATA
1860A334h R_BE_TXL_SRAM_WRITE_EN
[31:0] RW 0 B_BE_SRAM_WE
1860A338h R_BE_TXL_SRAM_CMD
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:29] RO 0 RSVD
[28] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_UL
[27:26] RO 0x0 RSVD
[25:24] R/W 0 B_BE_SRAM_ACID
[23] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_WR 0: read
1: write
[22:20] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_CMD target SRAM block
0: bitmap SRAM
1: length SRAM
2: DL RU SRAM
[19:10] RO 0 RSVD
[9:8] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_OFFSET if B_AX_SS_CMD = 0
0: MACID 0~31
1: MACID 32~63
2: MACID 64~95
3: MACID 96~127

if B_AX_SS_CMD = 1 or 2
32 bit offset, 0 for bit 0~31, 1 for bit 32~63, 2 for bit 64~95, 3 for bit 96~127
[7:0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SRAM_MACID if B_AX_SS_CMD = 0
0,1: macid_pause
2,3: p2p_group_0
4,5: p2p_group_1
6,7: delay_Tx_en
8,9: WMM0 STA bitmap
10,11: WMM1 STA bitmap
12,13: WMM2 STA bitmap
14,15: WMM3 STA bitmap
16,17: twt_group_0
18,19: twt_group_1
20,21: twt_group_2
22,23: twt_group_3
24,25: ul_sta_bitmap
26,27: BSR_≧_thr
28,29: RMAC UL bitmap
30,31: BSR threshold sel

if B_AX_SS_CMD = 1
MACID, 0=MACID0, 1=MACID1, ......

if B_AX_SS_CMD = 2
DL RU group, 0 = group0, 1 = group1,
1860A33Ch R_BE_READ_LINK_INFO
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:29] RO 0x0 RSVD
[28] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_UL 1: UL link
0: WMM link
(linkid == 'd17) UL1, (linked=='d16) == UL0
[27:26] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_WMMID 0/1/2/3: WMM0/1/2/3, for WMM link
[25:24] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_ACID 0/1/2/3: BE/BK/VI/VO, for WMM link
[23:16] RO 0xFF B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_LENGTH number of MACID in this link
[15:8] RO 0xFF B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_TAIL tail MACID of this link
[7:0] RO 0xFF B_BE_FW_RD_LINK_HEAD head MACID of this link
1860A340h R_BE_ADD_STA
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:29] RO 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_ST
[28] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_UL 1: UL link
0: WMM link
[27:26] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_WMMID 0/1/2/3: WMM0/1/2/3, for WMM link
[25:24] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_ACID 0/1/2/3: BE/BK/VI/VO, for WMM link
[23:18] R/W 0x0 RSVD
[17:9] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_MACID1 the 2nd MACID to add
if do not need it, this field should be 0x1FF
[8:0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_ADD_STA_MACID0 the 1st MACID to add
if do not need it, this field should be 0x1FF
1860A344h R_BE_DEL_STA
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:29] RO 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_ST 0: success
1:
2:
3:
[28] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_UL 1: UL link
0: WMM link
[27:26] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_WMMID 0/1/2/3: WMM0/1/2/3, for WMM link
[25:24] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_ACID 0/1/2/3: BE/BK/VI/VO, for WMM link
[23:18] R/W 0x0 B_BE_SS_RSVD_44_23_18
[17:9] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_MACID1 the 2nd MACID to delete
if do not need it, this field should be 0x1FF
[8:0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_DEL_STA_MACID0 the 1st MACID to delete
if do not need it, this field should be 0x1FF
1860A348h R_BE_FORWARD_SEARCH_STA
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_STA_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:29] RO 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_FWD_ST 0: success
1:
2:
3:
[28] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_STA_UL 1: UL link
0: WMM link
[27:26] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_STA_WMMID 0/1/2/3: WMM0/1/2/3, for WMM link
[25:24] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_STA_ACID 0/1/2/3: BE/BK/VI/VO, for WMM link
[23:20] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_TWT_GROUP per bit mapping to a TWT group
1: in group
[19] RO 0 B_BE_SS_RSVD_48_19
[18] R/W 0 B_BE_FW_SRH_P2P_SEL select band0/1 ptcl_p2p_win to use
[17:16] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_TWT_MODE definition is the same as F2P_CMD.SS_SEL_MODE
[15:9] RO 0 RSVD
[8:0] R/W 0x0 B_BE_FW_SRH_STA_MACID the MACID to be primary STA
if 0x1FF: FW do not assign MACID, HW auto search
1860A34Ch R_BE_COMMAND_REG
[31] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_CMD_OWN trigger: write 1 to trigger HW
HW done: polling to 0
[30:26] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_SS_CMD_SEL
[25:8] $R/W 0x0 B_BE_SS_VALUE [30:26]: cmd_sel / status
0: read setting_quota, [11:8]
1: write setting_quota, [11:8]
2: read quota, [16:8]
3: write quota, [16:8]
4: decrease quota, [15:8]
5: read Tx length, [28:8]
6: add Tx length, [23:8]
7: decrease Tx length, [23:8], if 0xFFFF => set to 0
8: read DL MU/RU disable, [9]: RU, [8]: MU
9: write DL RU/MU disable, [9]: RU, [8]: MU
10: read UL gid_vld_tab, [22:8]
11: write UL gid_vld_tab, [22:8]
12: read BSR length, [22:8]
13: write BSR length, [22:8]
20: read next link, [15:8]
21: write next link, [15:8]
22: read pre link, [15:8]
23: write pre link, [15:8]
if B_AX_SS_CMD_SEL = 13:
HW update this field to AC_TYPE of BSR
else if B_AX_SS_CMD_SEL = 12:
HW update AC_TYPE of BSR to this field
else:
0/1/2/3: BE/BK/VI/VO
*when trigger:
if B_AX_SS_CMD_SEL = 0~4 or 20~23:
[23] = 1: UL path (only for quota and link)
[23] = 0: AC path (for quota, link and Tx length)
else:
value to write/add/decrease
*when HW done:
read value for read CMD
UL1 RD LINK
[7:0] RW 0x0 B_BE_CMD_MACID MACID

1860B000h R_BE_HAXI_INIT_CFG1
[31:28] R/W 4'b0 B_BE_CFG_WD_PERIOD_IDLE PCIE WD-DMA interval of idle mode (TXON is low). 0: no delay. 1: 256. 2: 512. 3: 768. 4: 1024. 5: 1536. 6: 2048. 7: 4096. 8: 8192. 9: 16384. 10~15: rsvd. Unit: ns.
[27:24] R/W 4'b0 B_BE_CFG_WD_PERIOD_ACTIVE PCIE WD-DMA interval of active mode (TXON is high). 0: no delay. 1: 256. 2: 512. 3: 768. 4: 1024. 5: 1536. 6: 2048. 7: 4096. 8: 8192. 9: 16384. 10~15: rsvd. Unit: ns.
[23:20] RO 4'b0 B_BE_RSVD
[19] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_EN_RO_IDX_UPD_BY_IO 1: Enable tx/rx dma_idx and rx close tags can update by write register.
0: disable.
[18] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_RST_KEEP_REG Keep register
[17] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_FLUSH_HAXI_MST Write 1 to flush read/write data channel and auto clear
[16] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_SET_BDRAM_BOUND Write 1 to reset TXBDRAM
[15] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_ADDRINFO_ALIGN4B_EN Address Info Format
1: 4B align mode ( fully support 256 GB range)
0: normal mode ( support 64GB range)
[14:13] R/W 2'b0 B_BE_RXBD_DONE_MODE 2'b00: write bdinfo at rx buffer start address per rxbd done
2'b01: write bdinfo at rxbd start address per rxbd done
2'b10: Don't write bdinfo per rxbd done
2'b11: Don't write bdinfo per rxbd done
[12:11] R/W 2'b0 B_BE_RXQ_RXBD_MODE 2'b00, 2'b10: PCIE RXDMA use Packet Mode
2'b01, 2'b11: PCIE RXDMA use Separation Mode
2'b10: PCIE RXDMA use RXBD Extension Mode
[10:8] R/W 3'b0 B_BE_DMA_MODE 3'b000: PCIe mode without Data CPU
3'b001: PCIe Cut-Through mode with Data CPU
3'b100: USB mode
3'b110: SDIO mode
3'b111: GMAC mode
[7] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_AXI_MST Stop HAXI master
[6] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_RXDMA_ALIGN64B_EN 1: Enable tx/rxdma read/write boundary 64B align mode
0: Disable
e.g. Write start address = 0x30 with 0x80 bytes
RXDMA will split it into 2 write cmds,
cmd1: wr_addr=0x30 with length = 0x10;
cmd2: wr_addr=0x40 with length = 0x70;
[5] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_RXDMA_EN 0: Stop RXDMA for all channel.
1: enable RXDMA for all channels.
When value = 0 asserted, RXDMA will stop after finishing to move packet state machine
[4] R/W 1'b0 B_BE_TXDMA_EN 0: Stop TXDMA for all channel.
1: enable TXDMA for all channels.
When value = 0 asserted, TXDMA will stop after finishing to move packet state machine, then reset pre-fetch.
[3:2] R/W 2'b01 B_BE_MAX_RXDMA rxdma write burst length :
0: 64 bytes; 1: 128 bytes; 2: 256 bytes
others: 128 bytes.
[1:0] R/W 2'b10 B_BE_MAX_TXDMA txdma write burst length :
0: 64 bytes; 1: 128 bytes; 256 bytes; others : 256 bytes
1860B004h R_BE_HAXI_DMA_RXQ_APPLEN
[31:14] RO 18'b0 RSVD RSVD
[13:0] R/W 14'b0 B_BE_RXDMA_EXT_LEN rxq part1 append length
1860B008h R_BE_HAXI_TRIG_PCIE_L0
[31:4] RO 28'b0 RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_AXIDMA_TRIG_PCIEL0_LTR_EN Set 1 to enable this condition to trigger pcie stays in L0: ltr request
[2] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_AXIDMA_TRIG_PCIEL0_WP_EN Set 1 to enable this condition to trigger pcie stays in L0: wpfifo_non_empty
[1] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_AXIDMA_TRIG_PCIEL0_RX_EN Set 1 to enable this condition to trigger pcie stays in L0: rxdma_idle
[0] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_AXIDMA_TRIG_PCIEL0_TX_EN Set 1 to enable this condition to trigger pcie stays in L0: txdma_idle
1860B010h R_BE_HAXI_DMA_STOP1
[31] R/W 0 B_BE_STOP_WPDMA Stop WP-DMA
[30:15] RO 16'b0 RSVD RSVD
[14] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH14 Stop CH14 H2D_CMD DMA
[13] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH13 Stop CH13 BMC DMA
[12] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH12 Stop CH12 FWCMDQ DMA
[11] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH11 Stop CH11 HIQ Band 1 DMA
[10] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH10 Stop CH10 MGQ Band 1 DMA
[9] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH9 Stop CH9 HIQ Band 0 DMA
[8] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH8 Stop CH8 MGQ Band 0 DMA
[7] R/W 1'b1 B_BE_STOP_CH7 Stop ACH7 DMA
